name: Gov. Roy Cooper
subject: My thoughts on North Carolina
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
A ray of light.

|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96689584/510850335/823540626?refcode=ea.09a182b7-679c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ms=ea.09a182b7-679c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOWExODJiNy02NzljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MEo_lLaTRiUdTCEN7Ws_9HByscZfj4wUsC9zKM6q9ME=&emci=224a41c6-549c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=09a182b7-679c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=1049176)
Hello Peter, I am deeply grateful to everyone who joined me in the effort to elect Democrats across the ballot. And while the Presidential race didn’t go the way we wanted, our fight wasn’t wasted. **We led with a positive vision for our state and we have the wins to show for it here in North Carolina.** We broke the state House Republican supermajority, which will help leverage good bills and help stop bad ones. We defeated their dangerous gubernatorial nominee, Mark Robinson, and elected **Josh Stein** as our next governor. Democrats held the Attorney General’s office, electing **Jeff Jackson** while rejecting Dan Bishop’s extremism. **Mo Green** , our nominee for Superintendent of Public Instruction, defeated his opponent — a QAnon conspiracy theorist who attended the insurrection on January 6th. We flipped the Lieutenant Governorship by electing **Rachel Hunt** and ensured **Elaine Marshall** will continue to lead as our Secretary of State. We reelected Congressman **Don Davis** in a swing Congressional seat helping to push the U.S. House toward a potential Democratic majority. This was a team effort, and we couldn’t have done it without supporters like you. There will be long days ahead of us, but we will never stop fighting for our country, our democracy, and our freedoms. Governor Roy Cooper [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96689585/510850336/-1724542601?refcode=ea.09a182b7-679c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ms=ea.09a182b7-679c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOWExODJiNy02NzljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=MEo_lLaTRiUdTCEN7Ws_9HByscZfj4wUsC9zKM6q9ME=)

name: Seth Magaziner
subject: I’m so grateful to have had you on my team
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY Magaziner for Congress
body:
Seth is in Congress to fight for the middle class and those working hard to join it. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://act.sethmagaziner.com/p/9_n7o1XMskam_GoKXFDAdg2?unsubscribedata=u%2BHJD8Muxg8Tf6gVzvjm9EncLLzikJOVfjlGrf3pahPEH%2BGsDOjOSvjsA3bd2rDddV7QMMuvnxhm2VnTL6%2FOsq1su8k%2B7EjXFJw1QHVb6dsObYBjzsF%2B%2BsUWQbP8gti8GxlxqVm6x12puuj%2BidkdJqOeraEl0NcxUzCNusZ2HfnzlafKhaZpPWyNrY84hrFeTqxWCWDZXVEY6mKE5jOafy2uTlcwpe4FBCsvERpS4p%2FxJ7tSHyuffUYRXhIns%2FdxZ27gs2ky9I01OxNLOrevMhqkXiNHPbyNwnHuoKuAPYNIjWtNBmE%2BNJ5sZLVP5fVITCcFDsVk8j2%2FpRvSXi5weW%2FTg7H2BoXJmAKysz%2BAnRABz5VTVk6o5TGGptBdzzbakP8O00pJy6tqw1I6vgEHV%2BB1B%2FL6zAXQaDnsfFVUMfVF4id9fhtCDYwxnGtu1h4i)
---
| |
---
Peter,

Thanks to generous supporters like you, we were successful in keeping this seat blue.

I’m so grateful to have had you on my team. Thank you again for your support — we truly couldn’t do this without you. With your help we grew our margin significantly since 2022 by relentlessly focusing on the needs of working people, and that is where I will keep my focus. I am proud of the work that we all have put in over the last two years and am honored to be re-elected to represent RI-02.

And while I am disappointed by the results of the Presidential election, I respect the electoral process and the choice of the American people.

I will work with any administration of either party when it is in the best interest of Rhode Islanders. But I also know that Rhode Islanders expect their representatives in Congress to always stand up to the executive branch when necessary to defend our democracy, rights, and freedoms. No matter what lies ahead, I am committed to fighting for Rhode Island values in Washington.

There is still a lot left to do, so let’s get to work.

Seth

name: James Brown
subject: Thank YOU!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JAMES BROWN FOR MONTANA—REPUBLICAN—LORNA KUNEY, TREASURERPO BOX 1585, HELENA, MT 59624
body:
WE did it! Yes, WE. I could not have won this race without you. Together, we sent a message and we are going to get to work right away! | | | | | [](https://gmail.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e42c6bb78e8b53de5ee6ec610&id=a8e5207f9b&e=80cd3574a3)
---
| | | | |
---
| Friend,
We won!
After a year of traveling the state, visiting with fellow Montanans, and spreading our message small government principles, the voters selected _our_ vision! I could not have done it without you and I am truly honored.
---
| Now the real work begins.
I plan on delivering on my promise to hold out-of-state corporations accountable, to tackle the challenges presented by white collar criminals using artificial intelligence as a means to scam our seniors, and of course, managing our public lands appropriately to help fund Montana’s K-12 education system.
There is a lot of work to be done, and I am ready to hit the ground running. I hope you will stay in touch and let me know how I can best serve you in the Auditor’s Office.
Thank you, again, for your support. Together, we are making a difference for Montana.
Sincerely, James BrownState Auditor—Elect
---

name: Harriet Hageman
subject: Thank you.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY HAGEMAN FOR WYOMING**
body:
From the bottom of my heart.
| | | |
---
**Friend,** from the bottom of my heart..
_**Thank you.**_
**This is Harriet Hageman** , and I couldn’t be more grateful for your support.
My race for Wyoming’s at-large congressional seat has just been called, but this victory is not just mine.

_**This is a victory for every single conservative patriot in America.**_

Thank you for the faith and trust you have placed in me to represent you in Congress, and I promise to you today that I will never give up the fight to protect our freedoms, liberties, and conservative agenda in Washington.

The road ahead to Make America Great Again won’t be easy, but with your support, we will continue to build a stronger, more prosperous future for every American.

Thank you again for your continued support, Wyoming. **_Let’s get to work!_**

Sincerely,
Harriet Hageman

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: Urgent update from Nevada:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada Rosen for Nevada
body:
This race, and its stakes, are higher than ever.
| | |
---
Here's where things stand, friend: Donald Trump is poised to win back the White House. **But** **Nevada’s Senate race is still too close to call – which means our work isn’t done yet. And we’re expecting my race to come down to just a handful of votes.**
With Donald Trump in the White House, we cannot afford to lose another Senate seat. If Republicans flip Nevada red, they’ll have even more power to plow through with their destructive agenda – gutting the Affordable Care Act, giving more tax breaks to big corporations, and even passing a nationwide abortion ban.
Winning is going to require ensuring that every last vote gets properly counted. name: Sean Feucht
subject: What I Saw At President Trump's Victory Party
body:
| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=53f2e634e3d34045a45cb8bc378a7ec1&_e=EPNtwPNYwLnhvKuKZMy9QjU98jorjnM5dHl-URHlwI3SmOLe_JvpKvteNYBZo6KmfxMKyd6klOesu15v9WZN83PhKRiD9QlluXx5eSXCmVk3QBZhwWorCVS_N1DVSIyZNuouVRt_nqq1PGO9E-3gfztXo7RlimAyiLoZ_-bALoRcLU6wkqou93RAh1qLTbzuxYUqsZ8DtZiYOzu8hflsJQTOMx7xfl-ttpEcQ9ME9NgaCeBJegfMoJa5urSvVYbhFHChKTyKAIoalUcxVMV9L3l9heQ3iVxfci63hZC8vR2xOnbpjnxD5rU-L0Mh0w7n)
---
Dear Friend,
---
Last night, my wife and I had the honor of being invited to Trump Campaign Headquarters to attend the victory party. And what a night it was! We witnessed history as we represented the millions of intercessors and prayer warriors around the world who helped fuel this historic victory.

For the past two years, we have brought revival and worship to all fifty state capitals and Washington D.C. We couldn't have done it without you, and last night we represented you. There was a spirit of joy that overwhelmed the room, and **[a 3:00 AM worship session broke out](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=53f2e634e3d34045a45cb8bc378a7ec1&_e=EPNtwPNYwLnhvKuKZMy9QgZZ1YWN-riF5hqyfyvW1VJWat0kIGeEh9xihpYagq7ZIDT2mxVgF4_hE3hXMB0S9bt0f9WF5WvhSSbh8yPBfeXn0u-k3YTrqwvUDWS4_msOKBNguff9v2Ek0IJ7t3_Ppg8rPszGjavQlOMf2po3FXcJsjon3ryQw8z-XDKArDuopBYnV3281KtFdJUthid49RxScBdkt8mJtQ44Z_cgYz_xfU_27bNOyXj-KZvCtw2cNYzBKoOddZQ6aQnVyQcj5lkulwHwWYRSbPVj5jgaFLkF1MEIVJYEdfZ6OmWL9J6g)** among the sea of politicians, campaign staffers, and political operatives.
---
---
After last night, two mandates are clear to me. First, a mandate from heaven confirms that the Lord hasn't abandoned our nation, and His favor has been graciously extended to America. And second, the American people delivered a mandate to President Trump to lead!
---
[**SUPPORT LET US WORSHIP >>>**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=53f2e634e3d34045a45cb8bc378a7ec1&_e=EPNtwPNYwLnhvKuKZMy9QjU98jorjnM5dHl-URHlwI3SmOLe_JvpKvteNYBZo6KmfxMKyd6klOesu15v9WZN83PhKRiD9QlluXx5eSXCmVk3QBZhwWorCVS_N1DVSIyZNuouVRt_nqq1PGO9E-3gfztXo7RlimAyiLoZ_-bALoTqw3m_m-aNPNVDGROs39CcdfmXpVBJLjyBilwTlra1mgazpjq8EfaFM1inNKR4JPfjpLK-DsOqwrBPwPNuOQNV05OGiznttZ-tCFqMtUDBHVjYOFgB8bK5Hqw2pvRZqRV24whCKuxM72KGTAcNnPT1)

Till all hear,
---
---
Sean

name: Gloria Johnson
subject: Thank you from the bottom of my heart.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gloria for Tennessee
body:
We may not have won, but the fight goes on.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96691444/510857054/-849459103?refcode=ea.da775b75-6a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ms=ea.da775b75-6a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYTc3NWI3NS02YTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b51R8hAew9uD7N-OTI6gIfLbBqiBHvoH_QlOxPG5V3w=&emci=c7c1b800-929b-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=da775b75-6a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=46764483)
---
I wanted to reach out personally to say thank you. Last night, the people of Tennessee made their voices heard, and while we didn’t get the result we hoped for, I am deeply thankful for the journey we took together. To everyone who stood with me in this campaign—knocking on doors, making calls, spreading our message, and showing up for each other—you’ve shown me what Tennessee’s strength truly looks like. I may not be able to take this fight to Washington, but I want you to know that I will never stop fighting for the people of Tennessee. The work we started together does not end here. Our voices, our vision, and our values will continue to shape the future we believe in. Thank you, from the bottom of my heart, for everything. We’ll keep pushing forward together, shoulder to shoulder, until Tennessee is a place where everyone can thrive. With all my gratitude, Gloria Johnson

name: Katie Porter
subject: I am going to fight for our country, will you join me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Truth to Power PAC
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/9?t=1&akid=39272%2E3422442%2EMBKpC7)
Derek, I won’t BS you. This is a tough day.
Here’s where we stand: Donald Trump has won the White House, and Republicans have gained control of the Senate.
BUT, Democrats still have a path to flipping the House of Representatives. Here’s where we stand as of 12:09 PM ET, with 218 seats needed for Party control:
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241106-2?refcode=em20241106-39272&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39272_3422442_MBKpC7&akid=39272%2E3422442%2EMBKpC7)
Winners have yet to be declared in several crucial swing districts. These too-close-to-call races include:
* CA-41 (Will Rollins),
* CA-45 (Derek Tran), and
* CA-47 (the district I currently represent, where Dave Min is running).

We always knew that control of the House would likely come down to swing districts in California. This is why we endorsed strong Democratic candidates here early and supported these campaigns’ GOTV efforts until the polls closed.
No matter what happens, one thing is clear: we need to be ready to fight Trump’s extremism and work every day to protect our rights.
**[I plan on doing everything I can to fight for our country’s future. I refuse to give in or give up. Will you join me by donating $5 right now to help us fight back against Trump?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241106-2?refcode=em20241106-39272&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39272_3422442_MBKpC7&akid=39272%2E3422442%2EMBKpC7)**
[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20241106-2?refcode=em20241106-39272&refcode2=39272_3422442_MBKpC7&akid=39272%2E3422442%2EMBKpC7)
Thank you so much for being a part of this movement,
Katie Porter

**Katie Porter started Truth to Power to support Democrats who reject corporate PAC money** and are running to protect the environment, restore the nationwide right to an abortion, and create an economy that works for working people. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/truth-to-power-1?refcode=em20241106-39272&amounts=&t=2&refcode2=39272_3422442_MBKpC7&akid=39272%2E3422442%2EMBKpC7)**
---

name: Greater Georgia
subject: LOEFFLER HIGHLIGHTS GOP GROUND GAME, CELEBRATES REPUBLICAN WINS
body:
| | | |
---
| FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE WEDNESDAY, NOVEMBER 6 LOEFFLER HIGHLIGHTS GOP GROUND GAME, CELEBRATES REPUBLICAN WINS
ATLANTA - Today, Former U.S. Senator Kelly Loeffler applauded the efforts of Greater Georgia, the Georgia GOP, the RNC, and other conservative groups after Republicans [won](https://fsdy8nbbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001fONhOfPl51tKE790lU80sgg9jTCaq2JCPuz7LEFaQo8nDD3T0_kIf64xyhliiQCPsqhPlmkYGWR-878cJ4MmRqwaaucpPlU9zaJiTZGKAGR7YgUvTwSvA9QACN0VDEaXKCS_TD16GRUsEYcS7MDY-Kw7jjczTPYdlk-BTxExUGqf8EkQQwSkUFUxTKgH6hx3BIHK20XoYpWagi868bKKUXt6X48lVk0s1NRQoiZZjD6wRWlXlmJTjj0gNDbLkL5BfZHYRgptqSxVCbm0AgR21g==&c=xW15FWBSizMgmkHwdSeWlIjrapX129Xx73sscHtzw2uMNAfTOeZnYw==&ch=SJzwvvrwclre9QrYkXAH6zVIQanSsLQVx6MwzVDT2HK0ciiPO8xfxQ==) decisive victories up and down the ballot across the state. President Trump won with a record-breaking 2.65 million votes, the most ever received by a Republican candidate in Georgia. Republicans in the State Senate maintained their 33-23 majority and Republicans in the State House will also preserve their majority, having defended three key tossup districts and flipped one Democrat-held seat. "I founded Greater Georgia to apply the strategic lessons we learned in the 2020 election, with the singular mission of creating a winning conservative infrastructure in our state. Over nearly four years, we have invested millions and worked every day to drive voter registration, diverse community outreach, and election integrity, building a new conservative coalition and solidifying Georgia as a red state," said Greater Georgia Chairwoman Kelly Loeffler. "Last night's victories, from the state legislature to the presidency, were years in the making. They are a testament to the candidates, campaigns, and preparation of groups like Greater Georgia, the Georgia Republican Party, the Republican National Committee, and other key partners. >From the ground game to election integrity, 2024 was a massive step change for conservatives despite being outraised at every level.
"We're already evaluating the data to find ways to sustain these gains for the long term - and I look forward to collaborating with our conservative counterparts to build on this solid foundation to engage Georgia voters and keep our state red." Leading up to this election, Greater Georgia invested over $1M in voter registration and several get-out-the-vote campaigns, making more than 14,000,000 voter contacts. Greater Georgia targeted - and saw gains from - the following conservative-leaning groups:
* Black, Hispanic, and Asian voters
* Voters aged 18-29
* South Georgia voters impacted by Hurricane Helene
* Low-propensity male voters in North Georgia
* Conservative and swing voters in Fulton County

---
| Greater Georgia, launched by former U.S. Senator Kelly Loeffler in 2021, is a 501(c4) nonpartisan nonprofit dedicated to growing participation in the democratic process by mobilizing and empowering voters. The group works year-round to educate and register voters, engage with diverse and underrepresented communities, and protect election integrity. ###
Media Contact: [press@greatergeorgia.com](mailto:press@greatergeorgia.com)
---

name: Alexandra Rojas
subject: Democratic leadership failed to stop Trump
disclaimer_text:
paid for
by billionaires and corporations that are dividing our communities,
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter, the 2024
election is over, and Donald Trump has officially won the presidential
election.

Trump’s victory is a devastating outcome for our country and the
innumerous marginalized communities he has targeted for the last decade.

There are millions of people living in the U.S. who are scared right now
for what the future holds and we stand ready to protect and defend each
other. We face threats of mass deportations, a national abortion ban, and
an all-out assault on our democracy and civil rights. Our political
leadership – those who have had the power and privilege to govern over us
for decades – owes it to each of them to take responsibility for how a
second Donald Trump presidency became possible again under their watch.

The leadership of the Democratic Party has repeatedly chosen to let Trump,
the GOP, and corporations dictate our policy positions rather than our own
base or the people who will inherit the future we are fighting for.

Our leadership cannot say they stand for peace and the sanctity and
prosperity of all lives if we continue to let families get forced out of
their homes because they can’t make ends meet in this economy; throw away
our asylum policies because of Trump’s demonizing of immigrants; or refuse
to stop funding bombs we know are wiping out entire generations of
families in Palestine.

The Democratic Party is rapidly losing its legitimacy amongst the everyday
people and marginalized communities continuously used as stepping stones
to win elections. For as long as our Party has cozied up with corporate
CEOs, right-wing billionaires, and big money Super PACs, everyday people
in this country have seen Democrats’ populist platitudes as hypocrisy at
best, and outright deceitful at worst.

The reality is that the leadership of both parties is bought and paid for
by billionaires and corporations that are dividing our communities,
undermining our fundamental freedoms, exploiting our workers, and
destroying our planet. These monied interests are on the frontlines of
destroying our democracy, taking away the power of voters through their
unprecedented spending in elections — while those in power refuse to stand
up and fight back. For eight years, the Democratic Party has been the
Party against Trump instead of the Party for the people.

With the victory of Donald Trump, there are no easy answers for where we
as a country and movement go from here. But what is clear to us is that
politically courageous leaders at the federal level are needed now more
than ever – the Democratic Party must usher in a new era, led by new
leaders, not beholden to corporations and billionaires. Leaders in
Congress like the Squad have shown us another way of doing politics is
possible and represent the promise of uniting our fractured nation into a
multiracial democracy where everyone thrives and no one is left behind.

It’s time to end the era of career politicians and the corrupt campaign
finance laws that keep them in power. Either they can choose to step down
and welcome a new generation of teachers, nurses, union workers, and
everyday people to the halls of power or they can be forced out at the
ballot box. Their time is up.

In solidarity,

Alexandra Rojas
Executive Director

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20241106-36129-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36129_1203867_91tppg&akid=36129%2E1203867%2E91tppg

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36129%2E1203867%2E91tppg

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=4489%2E35263%2EEshCiM)
_We did it, Peter._ **The votes have been counted and I’m incredibly honored the people of Wisconsin have elected me to continue serving our state in the United States Senate.** I want to thank you, Peter, because I couldn’t have done it without you. I’ve been written off many times before, but this team of grassroots supporters helped us defy the odds once again. You and hundreds of thousands of supporters stood by me as Republicans tried to buy their way to victory. The opposition we faced was _historic_ — no Wisconsin Senate race has ever seen as much spending as this one. Together, we went everywhere and talked to everyone about the issues that matter most in our communities — and our work to deliver results so we can continue to move Wisconsin _forward._ Whether you made phone calls, knocked on doors, chipped in your hard-earned dollars, or simply kept up with our campaign updates, I can’t express how grateful I am for your support. Now, I know the results from last night weren’t everything we’d hoped for, and a lot of work still lies ahead of us — but for now, I wanted to make sure you heard directly from me about our hard-fought and hard-won Senate race. None of this would’ve been possible without you standing by my side, and I’ll do everything I can to make Wisconsin proud. Thank you, Tammy Baldwin
---
P.O. BOX 696 MADISON, WI 53701
**[www.tammybaldwin.com](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=2&akid=4489%2E35263%2EEshCiM) | [INFO@TAMMYBALDWIN.COM](mailto:INFO@TAMMYBALDWIN.COM) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/68?t=3&akid=4489%2E35263%2EEshCiM)**
PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
---

name: Thomas Carle
subject: Not the Outcome We Had Hoped For!
body:
By now - we have all witnessed the heartbreak of the election with few
bright spots to cling to and many of us just watched Kamala's conciliatory
address. She was great, as usual - still a sad day when we think of what
could have been.

Fortunately, as Kamala alluded to - the spirit and promise of America live
on, and there is work to do to keep those fires burning! So, mourn a bit,
tend to our wounds, then let's get going again!

We have causes to promote and defend.
We have groups to organize better for the next election cycle.
We have committees to join and get involved with.
We have to attend events and prepare for others.
We need to be thinking of who can run for what office and competing for
seats in the next election - finding potential candidates and helping them
evolve!
And we always have people in our community to lend a hand to.

We cannot let one loss define us.

A close friend of mine wrote a consolation speech for me, which sums up
where I am at today....
Like many of you, I started this adventure with the objective of standing
up for my friends and neighbors against the people who I perceived were
trying to divide us. We can have differences of opinion on many subjects;
from the best way to govern, the best way to raise our children, the way we
worship, or the kinds of music we like. However, when people in government,
or people in the media formulate alternative facts and misrepresent their
positions in order to sow dissension and confusion, or engage in outright
lies simply to gain power over the people that they wish to represent, we
have strayed too far from the principles that unite us.

I believe that as Americans, we all want certain basic rights. We all want
the right to live as we please, as long as we don’t infringe on the rights
of others. We all want the opportunity to earn a decent living, where our
children can grow and prosper, and be safe and healthy. We all want the
elders in our lives to be able to retire and live in dignity, receiving all
of the benefits that they worked so hard to earn. We all want to feel safe
in our homes, and want to feel that our country is safe in the world. We
all want the right to worship in whatever religion we choose, or none at
all. We all have more in common with each other than things we disagree
about. We have to get back to working together.

I got into this race because I think that my opponent and his party do not
believe this is true.

I have made my argument to all of the people of District 23, to the best of
my ability. Now, the voters have decided.

I accept that, with this caveat. I believe my opponent must be held to a
higher standard than he met in his first term in office. I believe that we
must unite behind him and give him the support he will need to be a
Representative who puts the interests of District 23 ahead of the interests
of the MAGA agenda that he has followed so far.

We need to support him in putting Country over Party. We must show him that
we will support him when he represents us properly, and that we will be
watching his actions and hold him accountable when he lets us down.

When I was asked by WENY and WSKG today, if I was going to run for this
office again, I replied, "it depends on how well the elected Congressman
performs, and how well the administration supports District 23."

Going forward, we must conduct ourselves with energy and civility, in
politics and in our daily lives, and be the people that we would like to
see in government. We must reject the tribal “us vs them” mentality that
leads to hatred and division. We need to set the tone for our nation.

I am willing to do this. To help, to learn and to monitor the performance
of our elected representative and I hope that all of you who worked on or
followed this campaign will do so, as well.

We need the energy to build our party up across the district and engage in
every opportunity to fill an elected seat. And our civility can be a
cornerstone for all of us.

Please find your own way of building our party and supporting others. As
we saw yesterday, it is always possible to fall just short of your goal -
so, let's look to the future and prepare for the outcomes that we truly
seek!

Thank you and regards to all, and have a terrific holiday season!

Tom

PS - please help to collect and/or recycle our campaign yard signs, if you
can. I will be making a trip through every location that I am advised of
for pickup...since I may be needing the signs again some day....thank you!

Divine Favor: Trump’s “Unprecedented, Powerful Mandate” Stuns Harris as Americans Overwhelmingly Reject Democratic Policies (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2024/11/divine-favor-trumps-unprecedented-powerful-mandate-stuns-harris-as-americans-overwhelming-reject-democratic-policies.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=51f1f8ecdd-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

President Donald Trump gave a victory speech to supporters in West Palm Beach on Nov. 5. (USA Today video)
By Illinois Review

President Donald Trump – the 45th and now 47th President of the United States stunned the political elites on Tuesday night – winning both the electoral college and popular vote – marking one of the greatest political feats in history as Vice President Kamala Harris underpreformed in every county across the country.

“Many people have told me that God spared my life for a reason. And that reason was to save our country,” President Donald Trump told the crowd full of supporters packed into the Palm Beach County Convention Center during the early morning hours on Wednesday. “This will forever be remembered as the day the American people regained control of their country.”
CLICK TO READ FULL ARTICLE (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2024/11/divine-favor-trumps-unprecedented-powerful-mandate-stuns-harris-as-americans-overwhelming-reject-democratic-policies.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=51f1f8ecdd-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

http://www.facebook.com/illinois.review?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=51f1f8ecdd-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D Facebook (http://www.facebook.com/illinois.review?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=51f1f8ecdd-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=51f1f8ecdd-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D IllinoisReview.com (https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=51f1f8ecdd-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
So far, Trump has 277 electoral votes while Harris sits at 224. 270 was needed to win, and Trump is expected to gain even more votes after Arizona, Nevada and Michigan finalize their results. Trump also won 51 percent of the popular vote in a stunning and shocking upset.

In Illinois, Trump was only 418,000 votes short of winning the Democratic-controlled and very blue state. In 2020, Trump lost the state by 1.25 million votes to Joe Biden, but this election was a different story where he outperformed both Harris and Biden across the board.

Harris proved to be a terrible candidate and Democrats will be second guessing their strategy of replacing Biden with her at the last minute. CNN anchor Jake Tapper was left stunned when shown a map on live television that Harris didn’t outperform Biden in a single county in the country.

“Holy smokes! Literally nothing? Literally not one county?”

(USA Today video) By Illinois Review President Donald Trump – the 45th and now 47th President of the United States stunned the political elites on Tuesday night – winning both the electoral college and popular vote – marking one of the greatest political feats in history as Vice President Kamala Harris underpreformed in every county across the country. “Many people have told me that God spared my life for a reason. And that reason was to save our country,” President Donald Trump told the crowd full of supporters packed into the Palm Beach County Convention Center during the early morning hours on Wednesday. “This will forever be remembered as the day the American people regained control of their country.” CLICK TO READ FULL ARTICLE () Twitter () Facebook () IllinoisReview.com () So far, Trump has 277 electoral votes while Harris sits at 224. 270 was needed to win, and Trump is expected to gain even more votes after Arizona, Nevada and Michigan finalize their results. Trump also won 51 percent of the popular vote in a stunning and shocking upset. In Illinois, Trump was only 418,000 votes short of winning the Democratic-controlled and very blue state. In 2020, Trump lost the state by 1.25 million votes to Joe Biden, but this election was a different story where he outperformed both Harris and Biden across the board. name: The COURIER Newsroom Team
subject: Don’t give up, Peter – we aren’t
body:
Today is a hard day – but we aren’t giving up.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96693194/510863210/2130526953?refcode=emwrapper&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMmY2NDk2ZDItNzA5Yy1lZjExLTg4Y2UtMDAwZDNhOThmYTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=2HopJInTcg9zbGtcAt6k4VN8iHoWO6RAhXgPomp85NA=&emci=adbf7bc6-6b9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=2f6496d2-709c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=14016711)
Peter **—**
We founded COURIER to give local communities a trustworthy, pro-democracy news source—so people have the truth to fight back against the lies and hate tearing our country apart.
Last night, sadly, lies and hatred prevailed.
**Like you, we’re devastated and deeply worried about what a second Trump presidency means for our country and the world. This moment is especially hard for women, immigrants, people of color, trans people, and _anyone_ who woke up feeling like a second-class citizen.**
Today is a difficult day, Peter. And while we can’t know exactly what comes next, we want you to know:
**We’re not giving up, and we’re not going away.**
In a time when legacy media have too often enabled Donald Trump’s lies, we believe independent local journalism is our best hope to fight back. We believe that to defeat fascism and heal our divisions, we must have the courage to stand up for what’s right and speak the truth—no matter how uncomfortable that truth might be.
**As COURIER’s Mark Jacob wrote today:**
_“We can’t hide from the disaster of these times. We ignore it at our peril. We have to fix it.”_
We’re grateful for everything you’ve done and continue to do to support COURIER and our democracy. We’re honored to continue this fight with you by our side.
In solidarity,
**The COURIER Newsroom Team**

subject: Nevada’s Senate race is too close to call
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate
body:
We can’t afford to lose another Senate seat. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96693493/510864521/-272948131?refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYWI0OTg1Mi03MTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fyNNZd6zCEQLz9YMSgWmIC7EkLQm2dsc0A4gZQo_fXk=&emci=148a0c28-6a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=0ab49852-719c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=252045)
---
_Below is a message from Jacky Rosen. Senator Rosen is running for re-election to the U.S. Senate in Nevada._
* * *
Here's where things stand, friend: **Nevada’s Senate race is still too close to call – which means our work isn’t done yet. And we’re expecting my race to come down to a handful of votes.** We cannot afford to lose a single additional Senate seat. If Republicans flip Nevada red, they’ll have even more power to plow through with their destructive agenda – including passing a nationwide abortion ban. Winning is going to require ensuring that every last vote gets properly counted. So today, I need to make the most important ask I've ever made of you: [**Please, will you split $10 now – or whatever you can spare – between my campaign and Martin Heinrich to show the huge wave of support we need to keep Nevada blue?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96693495/510864548/-982647225?amount=10&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYWI0OTg1Mi03MTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fyNNZd6zCEQLz9YMSgWmIC7EkLQm2dsc0A4gZQo_fXk=) | [DONATE $10](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96693497/510864556/-982647225?amount=10&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYWI0OTg1Mi03MTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fyNNZd6zCEQLz9YMSgWmIC7EkLQm2dsc0A4gZQo_fXk=)
---
We laid it all on the line to counter their attacks and turn Nevada blue. In order to do that, we had to spend every last dollar we had just to get through Election Day – because our race was one of the most expensive in the whole country.
Our work isn’t done until every vote in Nevada is counted. But today, we’re starting all over again from scratch, which is why we’ve set an emergency goal to raise $500,000. And now, the stakes are higher than ever – we absolutely need to reach this goal to win.
So please, friend, I'm counting on you to come through in the most pivotal moment: [**Split $10 or more right now to help my campaign get across the finish line.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96693500/510864568/-982647225?amount=10&refcode=AU_MTH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20241106_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYWI0OTg1Mi03MTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fyNNZd6zCEQLz9YMSgWmIC7EkLQm2dsc0A4gZQo_fXk=)

clean_body: We can’t afford to lose another Senate seat. Below is a message from Jacky Rosen. Senator Rosen is running for re-election to the U.S. Senate in Nevada. Here's where things stand, friend: Nevada’s Senate race is still too close to call – which means our work isn’t done yet. And we’re expecting my race to come down to a handful of votes.** We cannot afford to lose a single additional Senate seat. If Republicans flip Nevada red, they’ll have even more power to plow through with their destructive agenda – including passing a nationwide abortion ban. Winning is going to require ensuring that every last vote gets properly counted. So today, I need to make the most important ask I've ever made of you: = | = We laid it all on the line to counter their attacks and turn Nevada blue. In order to do that, we had to spend every last dollar we had just to get through Election Day – because our race was one of the most expensive in the whole country. Our work isn’t done until every vote in Nevada is counted. But today, we’re starting all over again from scratch, which is why we’ve set an emergency goal to raise $500,000. And now, the stakes are higher than ever – we absolutely need to reach this goal to win. So please, friend, I'm counting on you to come through in the most pivotal moment: = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will immediately be split between Jacky Rosen and Martin Heinrich: | = = | = = | = = Thank you so much. Jacky | Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate Senator Martin Heinrich is committed to helping New Mexico become a leader in defense, tech, and energy with opportunities in every corner. name: Lizzie Pannill Fletcher
subject: Yesterday, Today, and Tomorrow
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie-reelect-logo-outlined.png]
Yesterday was a day we had waited for, hoped for, and worked for, for years. And last night’s results were not the ones we hoped for, or worked for, in many races, including the race for the White House.
Today, I write to you with tremendous gratitude, however, for the hope and the work that you have done for the vision we share.
I am grateful to all the volunteers and the voters who have made it possible for me to do this work and to return to Washington for a fourth term representing Texas’ Seventh Congressional District. I am grateful to all the members of #TeamLizzie who read these messages and responded, contributing money and time to support this campaign and our vision. Our victory in TX-07 is a testament to the hard work, support, and generous contributions of #TeamLizzie. Thank you.
I am grateful to my dear friend Colin Allred for putting himself on the field and giving us the opportunity to vote for a Senator of high integrity and commitment to all Texans. And I am grateful to Kamala Harris and Tim Walz for their campaign to unify and support the American people with vision and purpose. They all made us proud.
Many of us are still processing last night’s results, and still waiting to know the outcomes in several House races that will determine who will control the House of Representatives. But we already know we have work ahead.
Tomorrow, I will go back to work, determined to continue the fight and do the work that led me to this campaign in the first place. Since 2018, our campaign has been about building a coalition of people who want to ensure that our community’s core values of inclusion, innovation, and collaboration result in progress, equality, and opportunity for all Americans. Together, we have done essential work for our community and our country.
Over the last two years of this election cycle, we’ve seen so many reminders of how much elections matter. How much leadership matters. And how much you matter. We have also seen time and again what we can accomplish when we work together. And we have a lot of work ahead.
I remain committed to doing the work every day, and to making you proud to be a part of #TeamLizzie.
Together, we can do anything,
LizzieSignature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]
3262 Westheimer, PMB 636, Houston, TX 77098
PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
Don't want to receive these emails anymore? unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/yiT4vmtXrUCBvy-D5k60rg2?unsubscribedata=%2B3ZNQERIadwjYeXCqHp5NneWivI5cftX7bJhvnPfUfxs9Rp3cbdBGmKN5Vop9ngm2pKnjpbJGBj%2FdhsLqMYKq9mb9Tq97mON%2Fpscu8ru0h83s6wSuSS0gXbOPq%2F5oLH5BBVI2OEsXfOVV%2BvNytib0cnpa9wo9znqOe94BhPCV8%2BDqo%2BoYM6WxppUHWcxbp1Ih92HLZ5cXcHaXoemSOgiDxp%2B6say3Z%2F2d8I9pXGzZ6e9t9364K%2FANGLqRJWtoI%2FgPR2sZIptSbUyV1dc%2FTgN6IQZnKtikAIoOBgl0e0z6SQDoxPVWuC715MRi3l8X11IMRKLZEH2iwXVkm2B1hYXbpoijzvBZmjEKnZrk3LF8VgpKjKDi87XTgjrpezTvK%2FJzccv%2BLesa%2Fa1MPZOjWX8AHJjMSpojCaIa0cpzIQoJ8knfel5xMtnn6eneXY9GmcV www.lizziefletcher.com [https://lizziefletcher.com/]

name: Ben Samuels
subject: Finding slivers of hope on a brutal night
body:
I'm trying to find *something* to be happy about from last night. You have to look pretty hard for it, but it's out there.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.Lf16T0DN6zF_aaxYHI9mN7tsA71oIz8Wpj7QsLdiWUE?) for more
---
# [Finding slivers of hope on a brutal night](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=151280471&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxMjgwNDcxLCJpYXQiOjE3MzA5MjAxMDMsImV4cCI6MTczMzUxMjEwMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.oJQ5dkY8Neivmk_Qzr8v6T_E1hefHdD-OxkxWSQTzyc)
### I'm trying to find *something* to be happy about from last night. You have to look pretty hard for it, but it's out there.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Nov 6
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
---
| | | [](https://substack..3h9uOxTWdFuQr-4ixMyqfj-Y240Bapo78vQ_IEgJDZQ?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/finding-slivers-of-hope-on-a-brutal-night?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---
Yesterday was brutal. There are no two ways about that.
I've gotten, by my quick count, about 200 texts from friends asking some version of the same question: is there _anything_ to feel good about?
I'm not going to linger on the topic of Trump--as friends of mine who were with me can attest, I did enough doomscrolling last night for a lifetime. There's a lot to be concerned and scared about, but I don't need to offer another negative take--there are plenty of those already.1
In looking for something positive, I feel a little bit like Eric Idle, singing ["Always Look on the Bright Side of Life"](https://substack.com/redirect/ca3a8a72-f760-47ba-aa50-851ddfc97569?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as he faces his imminent death at the end of _Life of Brian_. But here we are.
Maybe this can be a pinch of optimism in an otherwise brutal 18 hours.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTUxMjgwNDcxLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzMwOTIwMTAzLCJleHAiOjE3MzM1MTIxMDMsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.Ja9cvnnPxJixP7oCAB9SOalgleoh_DYB1VBjXIjVFvM?)
### Some of the most extreme candidates lost
Kamala Harris lost in North Carolina, but there's _some_ good news coming out of the state:
* Democrat Josh Stein, who by all accounts is a fundamentally good person, [will be North Carolina's next Governor](https://substack.com/redirect/9a1c7981-0157-497f-aad4-ca6b1289f578?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). He beat Republican [Mark Robinson](https://substack.com/redirect/246379d4-0d14-4fe2-8042-29e845cc6181?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), whose Wikipedia page has sections that include "Antisemitic remarks," "Holocaust denial," and "Homophobic and transphobic comments."
* Republican Michele Morrow, who has a habit of [calling for the execution of public figures she doesn't like](https://substack.com/redirect/17a11330-c9a7-47bd-a07e-37056063c7ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [lost her race](https://substack.com/redirect/82ed795e-c5b0-4f58-8ab1-fd4132e78293?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for Superintendent of Public Instruction to Democrat Maurice Green.
* Republican Dan Bishop, who [likes to compare the Taliban to LGBTQ activists](https://substack.com/redirect/4d304c97-2929-466a-8bb5-dea6c55e06d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [lost his race](https://substack.com/redirect/d8104016-09d3-4290-ac12-cbff2c862668?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for Attorney General to Democrat Jeff Jackson.

That's not the only example of this. Arizona, which Trump will probably win, is nonetheless likely to repudiate Senate candidate Kari Lake, who [still claims that she won Arizona's gubernatorial election in 2022](https://substack.com/redirect/0ee72456-835d-4063-8739-29911b719a4f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). ([She didn't](https://substack.com/redirect/224c1c7d-6bdd-4649-ba7f-e38e244732ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).) Democrat Ruben Gallego will likely win that race.
There are other examples of this too. But it's good to see voters rejecting some of the most extreme candidates.
### Abortion access generally did well
Abortion-access ballot measures, including [Amendment 3 in Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/abe817b9-648a-4586-99bc-1ada90f8e40e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), passed in states around the country.2 On a night of bad news, this is worth celebrating.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=151280471&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxMjgwNDcxLCJpYXQiOjE3MzA5MjAxMDMsImV4cCI6MTczMzUxMjEwMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.oJQ5dkY8Neivmk_Qzr8v6T_E1hefHdD-OxkxWSQTzyc)
### A new model in Nebraska
When I encouraged people to [give money to Independent Dan Osborn a few weeks ago](https://substack.com/redirect/6b8922be-03df-402b-8712-d22decad108f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I did so acknowledging that he was an underdog despite the polling. Unfortunately, [he indeed lost](https://substack.com/redirect/edf784e5-2a0b-4cf1-8677-dc35c168e411?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
But as was the case [in Utah two years ago](https://substack.com/redirect/94e379fb-b741-4a98-94b9-1cf8c1a1d2ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Republicans are winning by surprisingly small margins when independents run and Democrats don't.
Osborn had no business losing by so little in such a Republican state. And maybe there's a model here going forward: we need more independents, running one-on-one against a Republican.
### Climate
Republicans winning is bad for climate, full stop. Against that framing, two thoughts here:
* Washington state [overwhelmingly rejected a repeal of its cap-and-trade program](https://substack.com/redirect/35ed0a2b-a956-4e59-bd95-e60af25adf64?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Measure 2117. This is good policy, and it demonstrates the political viability of cap-and-trade at a time when that was uncertain.
* I think Elon Musk is a uniquely destructive force in American politics, in so many ways. I believe he's a bigot. The fact that Trump is in his pocket is, in general, very bad for this country. But that corruption will probably mean more support for and development of electric vehicles in this country, [at a time when their approval rating is waning](https://substack.com/redirect/c30b0e97-3ed4-433d-869e-7777ec7ab99f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It's perhaps the worst, most outrageous possible way to get to reasonable policy.

### There are good people in elected office
There is no shortage of good people in elected office. Obviously there are plenty of bad people too. But we should acknowledge that there are still people in public service for the right reasons.
I don't need to list all of them, and there are some good people I'm still hoping will win in U.S. House races that haven't been called just yet.
I'll note, however, that at the start of 2025, St. Louis will be incredibly lucky to be represented by Wesley Bell and Nikki Budzinski. Both are good people and good legislators; both care about the region. Both will be able to work with Republican majorities to deliver for Greater St. Louis.
Not everyone in the country is as fortunate as we are to have such good representation.
### Maybe this forces a reset
This election wasn't close; Republicans will have won the popular vote for the first time in 20 years. Democratic messaging is not resonating with enough people. I don't have the answers for why that is. But if we're losing to Donald Trump consistently--and two-out-of-three is unfortunately consistent--then we probably need a full reboot.
A closer election might've led to tinkering. We need something much deeper than that if we want to compete in '26 and beyond.
### Final thoughts
I'll leave all of you with two messages.
First, to quote the great philosopher Daniel Simpson Day3: **["Don't get mad; get even."](https://substack.com/redirect/9841df3f-5a14-4ece-ba0a-3c53a1ad3d27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** If you're worried about the future like I am, find ways to get involved. Figure out the causes you care about and engage. Feel bad now, and then look ahead towards the future.
Second, and on a more serious note, there's a [quote](https://substack.com/redirect/d3e33e79-5c4f-426b-ae37-be54c8176d12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from [Clarence Darrow](https://substack.com/redirect/5ce4b63e-217a-48da-a5e4-01f5f0d8c5ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)4 that I think about a lot in evaluating my life: whether **" I have done something to help human understanding, to temper justice with mercy, to overcome hate with love."**
I've seen a lot of people in let's-figure-out-who-to-blame mode: white women or young men or Latinos or Black people or whoever. This isn't just wrong morally and wrong factually; it's ineffective, and it diminishes what our neighbors are feeling and what they're experiencing.
I'm hardly perfect on this dimension. But I will do more to engage with people who don't think like I do or vote like I do.
We all could use a bit more help with human understanding.
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will enjoy it.** (If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/ae299dab-c33c-4621-bcc4-495559623fc4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)_
1
For me personally, beyond the obvious of a Trump win and a Republican U.S. Senate, it's really upsetting that [ranked-choice voting](https://substack.com/redirect/492b3360-c9be-477b-93db-33f5664aae34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) didn't do well. And [Missouri's Amendment 2](https://substack.com/redirect/b17a8e20-0417-499d-a89d-07741ce17fa3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is on a razor's edge, but unfortunately [I think it will pass](https://substack.com/redirect/d885a3f1-877f-4e22-a59a-4f3734e3520d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). My prediction: the consequences will be devastating to an extent that it'll get repealed within the next decade.
2
They [also passed](https://substack.com/redirect/5f8d58bc-4e90-443e-b152-8bc96be198c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Arizona, Nevada, Montana, Colorado, New York, and Maryland.
3
Whereabouts unknown.
4
The speech was given in the defense of [Leopold & Loeb](https://substack.com/redirect/544fde4e-2ca5-42e8-a867-07f7466c90fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), of all things, almost exactly a century ago.
---

clean_body: I'm trying to find something to be happy about from last night. You have to look pretty hard for it, but it's out there. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more I'm trying to find something to be happy about from last night. You have to look pretty hard for it, but it's out there. | | | Nov 6 | | | | | | Yesterday was brutal. There are no two ways about that. I've gotten, by my quick count, about 200 texts from friends asking some version of the same question: is there anything to feel good about? I'm not going to linger on the topic of Trump--as friends of mine who were with me can attest, I did enough doomscrolling last night for a lifetime. There's a lot to be concerned and scared about, but I don't need to offer another negative take--there are plenty of those already.1 In looking for something positive, I feel a little bit like Eric Idle, singing as he faces his imminent death at the end of Life of Brian. But here we are. Maybe this can be a pinch of optimism in an otherwise brutal 18 hours. ? Some of the most extreme candidates lost Kamala Harris lost in North Carolina, but there's some good news coming out of the state: Democrat Josh Stein, who by all accounts is a fundamentally good person, . He beat Republican , whose Wikipedia page has sections that include "Antisemitic remarks," "Holocaust denial," and "Homophobic and transphobic comments." Republican Michele Morrow, who has a habit of , for Superintendent of Public Instruction to Democrat Maurice Green. Republican Dan Bishop, who , for Attorney General to Democrat Jeff Jackson. That's not the only example of this. Arizona, which Trump will probably win, is nonetheless likely to repudiate Senate candidate Kari Lake, who . (.) Democrat Ruben Gallego will likely win that race. There are other examples of this too. But it's good to see voters rejecting some of the most extreme candidates. Abortion access generally did well Abortion-access ballot measures, including , passed in states around the country.2 On a night of bad news, this is worth celebrating. A new model in Nebraska When I encouraged people to , I did so acknowledging that he was an underdog despite the polling. Unfortunately, . But as was the case , Republicans are winning by surprisingly small margins when independents run and Democrats don't. Osborn had no business losing by so little in such a Republican state. And maybe there's a model here going forward: we need more independents, running one-on-one against a Republican. Climate Republicans winning is bad for climate, full stop. Against that framing, two thoughts here: Washington state , Measure 2117. This is good policy, and it demonstrates the political viability of cap-and-trade at a time when that was uncertain. I think Elon Musk is a uniquely destructive force in American politics, in so many ways. I believe he's a bigot. The fact that Trump is in his pocket is, in general, very bad for this country. But that corruption will probably mean more support for and development of electric vehicles in this country, . It's perhaps the worst, most outrageous possible way to get to reasonable policy. There are good people in elected office There is no shortage of good people in elected office. Obviously there are plenty of bad people too. But we should acknowledge that there are still people in public service for the right reasons. I don't need to list all of them, and there are some good people I'm still hoping will win in U.S. House races that haven't been called just yet. I'll note, however, that at the start of 2025, St. Louis will be incredibly lucky to be represented by Wesley Bell and Nikki Budzinski. Both are good people and good legislators; both care about the region. Both will be able to work with Republican majorities to deliver for Greater St. Louis. Not everyone in the country is as fortunate as we are to have such good representation. Maybe this forces a reset This election wasn't close; Republicans will have won the popular vote for the first time in 20 years. Democratic messaging is not resonating with enough people. I don't have the answers for why that is. But if we're losing to Donald Trump consistently--and two-out-of-three is unfortunately consistent--then we probably need a full reboot. A closer election might've led to tinkering. We need something much deeper than that if we want to compete in '26 and beyond. Final thoughts I'll leave all of you with two messages. First, to quote the great philosopher Daniel Simpson Day3: If you're worried about the future like I am, find ways to get involved. Figure out the causes you care about and engage. Feel bad now, and then look ahead towards the future. Second, and on a more serious note, there's a from that I think about a lot in evaluating my life: whether " I have done something to help human understanding, to temper justice with mercy, to overcome hate with love." I've seen a lot of people in let's-figure-out-who-to-blame mode: white women or young men or Latinos or Black people or whoever. This isn't just wrong morally and wrong factually; it's ineffective, and it diminishes what our neighbors are feeling and what they're experiencing. I'm hardly perfect on this dimension. But I will do more to engage with people who don't think like I do or vote like I do. We all could use a bit more help with human understanding. Feel free to share this post with someone who will enjoy it.** (If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, .) 1 For me personally, beyond the obvious of a Trump win and a Republican U.S. Senate, it's really upsetting that didn't do well. And is on a razor's edge, but unfortunately . My prediction: the consequences will be devastating to an extent that it'll get repealed within the next decade. 2 They in Arizona, Nevada, Montana, Colorado, New York, and Maryland. 3 Whereabouts unknown. 4 The speech was given in the defense of , of all things, almost exactly a century ago. | | | (C) 2024 Ben Samuels 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

| |
[](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=29250c99e42847b1b4a0768c43324bed&_e=M9HtItmJneNePQz3iU6jLcHDuPwvAWygPv5PVBm8DZuN26omvH-fMdx1K8-P-Mr_uWY_vGd-fAEg3eESZQ7WuNPsUQvpS7oFP9vJ47unse49-A6oXrqJWxqB1RsKuZOjakOmdp5ZzQyA_XXPxhJxbJNo9AUX3OP1tYEMvuzK_sfErpT3tbe96W0OsADHBvojxynrIE9NgOSxeeImfIGis4tmgJ-0Zbrdw8CnEtRVCgbBtl7cgolBVyzTChY_iM558WVAr1uCU1VRvSsyzAtPyfxfZO3NndFhGZt-N1GSiFI9x-9SyykOL2go0oVDXoUqi2S6bhAQNLPM6x4y2FFZp6bziYHm7GghxFPbzxJQcqzMCEdTS56dEXDYLogLx42OiWgLnaIQ0XOeJ12mi3RTCg%3D%3D) Dear Friend,

The history of the Roman Republic is a captivating tale of achievement, political and military genius, and the perils of success. One that still impacts our world today.

To help tell this important story to Americans nationwide, we released our free online course, **" The Rise and Fall of the Roman Republic."**

**You can now receive your own DVD box set of "The Rise and Fall of the Roman Republic" when you make a tax-deductible gift of $100 or more in support of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty.**

And, for a limited time, we'll also send you a **free color map** of the Roman world at the end of the Republic. This map is a helpful companion to the course DVD and will illustrate many of the topics discussed in the lectures.
Simply use this secure link to reserve your DVD box set of **" The Rise and Fall of the Roman Republic," **along with the companion map today:

[**https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/roman-republic-dvd**](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=29250c99e42847b1b4a0768c43324bed&_e=M9HtItmJneNePQz3iU6jLcHDuPwvAWygPv5PVBm8DZuN26omvH-fMdx1K8-P-Mr_uWY_vGd-fAEg3eESZQ7WuNPsUQvpS7oFP9vJ47unse49-A6oXrqJWxqB1RsKuZOjakOmdp5ZzQyA_XXPxhJxbJNo9AUX3OP1tYEMvuzK_sfErpT3tbe96W0OsADHBvojxynrIE9NgOSxeeImfIGis4tmgJ-0Zbrdw8CnEtRVCgbBtl7cgolBVyzTChY_iM55NeUk1AXNjyXXhyeUhU2WtwCBTDJVVfkcA4lgQuGXhb-W2GMfJLoT4EGaZqRyWHWh1weI6hbv1IBf1Qvkj01cLn4QsQUgZALMVvJ-3aYcpQISOwzzSrt_Lx1opkQApP_rQHVKZxawCWYFZjecjucaoA%3D%3D)

By studying ancient Rome, you'll discover the conditions necessary to preserve a republic, and, conversely, how a republic may lose its way.

That's why we produced **" The Rise and Fall of the Roman Republic"** and why I hope you'll watch and share the course on DVD.

Thank you for helping to preserve liberty for future generations of Americans through education with Hillsdale College!

Best regards,

Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D.
Executive Director of Online Learning
Hillsdale College

name: Steve Kerrigan
subject: This one hurts, but we can’t lose faith
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Dear Peter-
The results of the election were not what any of us hoped for or expected. I understand the disappointment, uncertainty and deep concern that many feel this morning, because I feel it too.
I am struggling with wanting to scream at the dawn of this new morning while, at the same time, recognizing that in campaigns, sometimes the other side wins. I am torn by wanting to roll over and hope it all goes away while understanding that democracy is hard and its cornerstone -- despite having lived through the very opposite four years ago -- is accepting defeat with a peaceful transfer of power.
I am also having a hard time reconciling such a beautiful morning with the storm we experienced last night. But, as with every storm, there is much to do the day after, taking stock and repairing breaches, mourning losses and rebuilding.
Today, it is clear there is much work to do. That work must be conducted with a touch of humility. While we can be proud of our performance here at home, sending Senator Warren and our Democratic congressional delegation back to Washington and maintaining wide majorities in both houses of the state legislature, it is OK to recognize that we were not prepared for the outcome nationally. You can curse the storm, but it does no good. We cannot create the better nation we all seek if we fail to understand one another. As a party, we must be open to listening and finding the lessons from this defeat.
There will be time for all that. First and foremost, I know many of you are hurting and that pain is very real and needs its space.
An election was lost but the campaign was one I will always be proud to have waged together with you. This outcome does not change the fact that our values and our commitment to building a just and inclusive society are as important as ever.
This is a time to reflect, connect with loved ones and renew our dedication to the things that unite us: compassion, fairness and equal opportunity for all. Those things are still there, guiding us, and in need of our help now more than yesterday and tomorrow more than today. It is on mornings like this that we need to come together, support one another and push forward.
Because of people like you, I remain hopeful. You believe in America and our democracy even at the hardest of times when it feels like our politics do not believe in you.
Our nation has survived struggles and threats more grave than today’s but that doesn’t matter when the struggle is yours and the threat feels so very real.
On days like this, after an election like this, I know it is hard to do but I am going to ask you to believe again in the promise of America. We simply must maintain belief in our collective ability to make change – person by person, block by block, issue by issue – until our energy and passion breaks through and sweeps down the mightiest walls of oppression.
I can’t help but recall what Ted Kennedy said in the face of his own devastating loss in 1980 when he finished his speech with “for me, a few hours ago, this campaign came to an end, but for those whose cares have become our concern, the work goes on, the cause endures, the hope still lives and the dream shall never die.”
The dream we all worked for in this campaign is alive in all of us. It can only truly die when the last of us gives up the fight. We must hold on to faith in our democracy. Whenever tested, it endures and lights the way forward. Change does not always happen as we’d like, but each effort brings us closer to that brighter future. Let’s keep working towards that, engaging in our communities with an open ear to voices that yearn to be heard.
Thank you for your steadfast commitment. Together, we will continue this journey, lifting each other up and holding true to our shared vision for a stronger, more inclusive America.
In solidarity,
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Steve%20K%27s%20Signature.png]
Steve Kerrigan
Chair, Massachusetts Democratic Party
https://www.facebook.com/massdems [https://www.facebook.com/massdems] https://twitter.com/massdems [https://twitter.com/massdems] https://instagram.com/massdems [https://instagram.com/massdems] https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems [https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems]
Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
Massachusetts Democratic Party
11 Beacon St
Suite 410
Boston, MA 02108
United States

Total victory! Thanks to you, WE WON!

# **MESSAGE FROM PRESIDENT TRUMP**
|
---
|
---
We overcame obstacles that nobody thought possible.
It’s a political victory that our country has never seen before.
I want to thank the American people for the extraordinary honor of being elected your 47th president.
To every citizen of our country, here is my promise:
**_I will fight for you, for your family, and your future. Every single day I will be fighting for you with every breath in my body._**
**_I will not rest until we have delivered the strong, safe and prosperous America that our children deserve and that you deserve. This will truly be the golden age of America._**
Together, we're going to unlock America's glorious destiny. We're going to achieve the most incredible future for our people.
So thank you once again for electing me in a landslide victory.
We have a lot to work to do, but I know with you by my side, WE WILL MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!

clean_body: Total victory! Thanks to you, WE WON! | | | | | | MESSAGE FROM PRESIDENT TRUMP | | We overcame obstacles that nobody thought possible. It’s a political victory that our country has never seen before. I want to thank the American people for the extraordinary honor of being elected your 47th president. To every citizen of our country, here is my promise: I will fight for you, for your family, and your future. Every single day I will be fighting for you with every breath in my body. I will not rest until we have delivered the strong, safe and prosperous America that our children deserve and that you deserve. This will truly be the golden age of America. Together, we're going to unlock America's glorious destiny. We're going to achieve the most incredible future for our people. So thank you once again for electing me in a landslide victory. We have a lot to work to do, but I know with you by my side, WE WILL MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN! Thank you, Donald J. name: Gabe Amo
subject: Thank you
body:
https://www.gabeamo.com/ [https://www.gabeamo.com/]

Hi Friend,

Today is Election Day and the polls are OPEN!

The presidential election is a clear choice about our nation’s collective future. I’m supporting Vice President Harris because she will work to lower costs, advance an opportunity economy so everyone can succeed, protect reproductive freedom, and defend our democracy. Most importantly, Kamala Harris will be a President for all Americans.

We have to turn the page on nearly a decade of Donald Trump. We have to move past his problems, his grievances, and his enemies list. We must also prevent his divisive Project 2025 agenda from increasing costs and limiting opportunities to a wealthy few.

For those voting in Rhode Island, I hope I earned your support to continue serving as your voice in Congress. I’m proud of what we have accomplished to bring federal resources home and share our state’s values in Washington. I know I can do even more, especially if we’re in the House Majority. It has been an honor of my lifetime to represent you and I humbly ask for your support to keep working for you.

Please also join me in supporting Democrats in important races up and down the ballot in our state. If you haven’t already voted early, it’s essential that every one of us makes a plan to vote.

Polls will remain open until 8:00 PM in Rhode Island. Click here to look up your polling location and finalize your plan to vote. Here's the link: https://vote.sos.ri.gov/Home/PollingPlaces?ActiveFlag=2

Questions about voting? Before you go to the polls, click here to review the necessary information before going to your polling place. Here's the link: https://iwillvote.com/?state=RI

Every vote matters. With your vote, we can send Kamala Harris and Tim Walz to the White House, hold onto the Senate, and take control of the House to make Hakeem Jeffries our next Speaker.

Let’s make our voices heard today,

Gabe
--

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gabe-amo-for-congress-2] GET UPDATES [https://www.gabeamo.com/]
https://www.facebook.com/gabeamoforcongress [https://www.facebook.com/gabeamoforcongress] https://twitter.com/gabeamo [https://twitter.com/gabeamo] https://www.instagram.com/gabeamoforcongress/ [https://www.instagram.com/gabeamoforcongress/] https://www.gabeamo.com/ [https://www.gabeamo.com/]

Gabe Amo for Congress
P.O. Box 40457
Providence, RI 02940
United States

name: Kenton Ngo, Cedar Key Progress
subject: Democracy is not yet dead
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cedar Key Progress and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
| | | To our loyal supporters and fighters for democracy:
As founder of Free and Fair Democracy PAC and Cedar Key Progress, I write to thank the thousands of Americans like you who supported our independent expenditure advertising campaigns against Donald Trump and for Kamala Harris.
I thank you for your trust and your donations. We did not take your commitment lightly, and I won’t waste time with silver linings or spin.
**I apologize without reservation, and with sincere regret, that we have failed.**
We accept the verdict of the American people.
**Democracy, however, is not yet dead.**
We do not yet know what campaign promises and fever dreams of Trump’s most fascist supporters will actually come true, nor do we know how quickly.
But we will condemn them if they begin crushing dissent, issuing unlawful orders to our military, purging the civil service, stripping Americans of citizenship, tearing apart families, and interfering with our elections as they have promised.
**Democracies do not die in a single day, not on Election Day, not on Inauguration Day, but with a thousand cuts.**
**We will fight back in the elections of 2025, 2026, 2027, 2028, and beyond, because we will not obey in advance.**
We will flood Congress with millions of letters and calls. We will elect state and local leaders who will resist, and take back Washington before it’s too late.
I founded Cedar Key Progress and Free and Fair Democracy PAC because I am the first of my line to be born with the right of government by the consent of the people.
As the son of ethnic Chinese immigrants by way of Vietnam, I look back at all the generations that came before me - none who were born with the right to vote.
They came to the United States after living under Communism, unstable republics, warlords, and imperial edict.
**We owe it to those who came before us to build this nation to not give up the fight.**
We owe it to those who have spilled blood to demand universal suffrage and free and fair democracy.
Until now, I have not signed my name to emails like this, because I am not important.
Like you, I am an ordinary American.
But I want MAGA Republicans to know that I will never willingly give in - that there will be no compromise with fascism.
**We have a long, and difficult road ahead of us.**
**We will welcome anyone who wishes to join our movement to defend democracy, and wake up every morning demanding a better country for future generations.**
Cedar Key Progress and Free and Fair Democracy PAC spent over $400,000 in independent expenditures to stop Trump, and we will do a full analysis of how we can improve and serve you better.
But now is not the time to discuss what we did, toot our own horns, or participate in recriminations and infighting.
For now, let’s take care of ourselves, and our families, and pledge not to obey in advance.
Once again, I apologize for our failures.
I sincerely hope that you will continue the fight as we look to build a blue wave of resistance in the 2025 and 2026 elections.
**We will be with you, in the trenches for freedom, for democracy is not yet dead.**
For our freedoms,
Kenton Ngo

name: Michelle Vallejo
subject: While this chapter is closing, our work continues
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Michelle Vallejo for Congress
body:
If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/mAp0_dUZb0WOLyBKJJuqXw2?unsubscribedata=gBN3Qx8GKlx2kWRl%2BiPSTZDZAGNxlcpc4yjpQrEVcuP5P8n%2Ba2qWlsHydY5PPE9ovxmxNXFNBeW9ETtPKaFmDKX4MdU0wNnFJv59urTVMaB93BcAA4V05kH5kXbxMDNGlR44%2B4fgcxeO9RSZlK5z2YV9UUS8L0JZ0soa4yBobxXltwvg5BiRDlHn9b7jhDgvooJ1vZTva3FFuIEPPNlPoCOPvIRKTRjpYfg7GcMDKF3r4FqGk5RIRKia4Js87FwYCATOfmyVDk0%2Feb0jO0DRBBEfILTVqWZ0E7x4Nv9M8R3ASwjyAFFF7NIEYozi0HJ%2B%2BU8Mnj0STDipVjarZP3Ucpt%2B3g6JcJWvdV9G3iWz6gbhjzaxE9dhM9P5xj3TpkN4607XE%2BdTX0CSXwKmhnYC30fynhubG8dRrjQWePboB8MgP%2FDOgIjIHN8mcL3wsuld)

---
Hi Peter, it’s Michelle Vallejo. The votes in the election for Texas’ 15th Congressional district are in. While our campaign is now coming to an end, I just want to say thank you today. I am so proud of the movement we built, proud of the campaign we ran, and proud of the people of South Texas who fight so hard everyday for a better future for themselves and their families. I stepped up to run for Congress because I believe that South Texans deserve more — affordable health care, better education, better quality jobs, functioning and fair border and immigration policies, and the freedom for women to make their own decisions about their health. That’s what it’s going to take to get our economy back on track and strengthen our families. That’s why I want to say thank you to my supporters in South Texas. Although this chapter may be closing, our work continues. Together, we will keep pushing for a brighter, fairer future. Thank you from the bottom of my heart for your trust, strength, and unwavering dedication. I congratulate my opponent and hope she will work hard to honor the needs of our district. Thank you,
Michelle
* * *

We’re honored by everyone who has made this journey possible through your contributions, survey participation, voting, and prayers. https://click.updates.troydowning.com/?qs=171ad8a9945a911f247632ae60c4228b4520d826cc13f48a8622c88c075de7bea0e92ac09e1d55d1672483fd1ee345274ab98881e205a32d87e6a5d7f9fc7c4b We are also incredibly grateful to the people of Eastern Montana, who have entrusted me to fight for them in Washington, D.C. | | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates.
