name: Darrius (personal)
subject: fight for our better angels
body:
It is our time to take our communities and our future into our hands. Take today to grieve, but tomorrow get active. Put your name on a ballot, volunteer with local organizations, and begin to clean up your communities.
---

It is with a heavy heart, and — I must admit — tears in my eyes, that I write today grappling with a sense of profound sorrow and great loss, not only for Georgia's 8th Congressional District; but for the nation, as we come to terms with the reality of last night’s electoral results. We knew going into this cycle that the campaign would be a steep mountain to climb. We understood that there were going to be some tough decisions and a fight ahead of us. Yesterday this leg of that fight came to an end.

Firstly, I want to personally thank the over 100,000 individuals who came out in support of me and this campaign. You have given me the greatest gift of a lifetime and that is the prospect of serving this great district. **_I can never thank you enough, and my sincere congratulations to Representative Scott._**

Secondly, I would like to thank President Biden and Vice President Harris for their unwavering leadership of both the nation and our party. Democracy and the economy were the two biggest issues on the ballot, and we thank President Biden for all his hard work and diligence in fighting for the American people. Likewise, Vice President Kamala Harris put up an amazing fight and we couldn’t be prouder of joining her in that effort. **Vice President Harris was the first top-of the-ticket candidate to seriously invest in middle and South Georgia. The Harris-Walz campaign heard our cries and they delivered for us. And for that, I know that we are all eternally grateful.**

Now, as we look to the future of a second Trump presidency, the question before us is “Where do we go from here?” To that, my answer is “I don't know.” But what I do know is that we cannot be discouraged. It's OK for us to be down for the moment. It is ok for us to grieve what could have been. **But we must stand up.** We must evaluate where we fell short and see how we can regain a stable footing. And for that, we must look to history. I know that many of us are feeling lost today, feeling as if this is a low point in the story of America, but as a student of America’s past, I can say with full certainty that better days are ahead of us.

Time and again, we have had moments where it seemed like America was on the wrong path, however, if there are Americans who remember that shining city on a hill, who remember that this house cannot afford to be divided, who remember that we are still constructing that bridge into the 21st century, we will prevail. **While that pavement might have a few bumps along the way, the arc of this nation will continue to bend toward justice and we will continue to fight for our better angels ahead.**

With Election Day behind us, it is our turn to find a way forward. Though the future may seem grim, and the American dream seems like little more than a myth, we must persist.

I still stand here today optimistic for what lies ahead. It is our time to take our communities and our future into our hands. Take today to grieve, but tomorrow get active. Put your name on a ballot, volunteer with local organizations, and begin to clean up your communities.

This fight is not over, we are just beginning. If there is still breath in the bodies of those who seek a better future, then this is still our moment. _This is our time._ **Let us not fall down the pits of sorrow but stand up on the mountaintop top of hope.**

May God bless us all, and God bless America.
— Rev. Darrius Butler

name: Andrew Yang
subject: Well that was Rough
body:
Hello, I hope that you are doing well as we process Election Day results.

https://www.andrewyang.com
https://home.forwardparty.com/2024_election_night

Hello, I hope that you are gearing up for Election Day. I voted early.

What is going to happen on Tuesday? The first thing to know is that there’s a good chance we won’t know who won by the end of the night, so try to be patient.

Who is going to win? What’s fascinating is that I’ve gotten reports of confidence from both sides. Both sides think that they’re going to win. The Republicans are in “who is going to take what job” mode. And Democrats who are looking at the early votes cast are confident that they’re going to pull it out, albeit narrowly.

Their strategies are somewhat different. The Democrats are trying to get out their voters and leaning on field teams and organizers. It’s sensible given their monetary advantage.

Republicans are reaching out to low propensity voters who are Trump-aligned but haven’t participated recently – hunters are one example. Trump won in 2016 largely by getting new voters to show up. You might think that well has been drained but the Republicans are testing it out again.

“If the usual voters show up, the Democrats are confident they’re going to win. If new voters show up then Republicans think it’s going to be theirs,” is how one person put it.

I was on CNN this week trying to prod the Harris campaign. I thought that Trump was going to win for quite a while – Kamala’s main path is to win Michigan, Wisconsin and Pennsylvania. The sunbelt states of Arizona, Nevada, Georgia and North Carolina seem to be slightly tougher terrain for her and better for Trump.

I thought that passing up Joe Rogan was a missed opportunity, personally. It’s a massive audience that’s predominantly male – kind of a gold mine.

The expectation is that the Republicans will win control of the Senate – they only need to pick up one seat aside from West Virginia – and the Democrats are slightly favored to win the House. In addition to the results that most people will be watching, I’ll of course be seeing how the Forward-endorsed candidates (https://home.forwardparty.com/endorsements) fare as well as the ballot initiatives to open up the primaries across the country. This year I’ll be heading to Forward Party’s Election Night (https://home.forwardparty.com/2024_election_night) event in Philadelphia – it should be a phenomenal gathering.

Will it be festive or funereal? We’ll all find out soon enough. As always, do what you can.

If you’d like to donate to Forward so we can have better choices in our politics, click here (https://home.forwardparty.com/donate) . Try to keep a level head, we'll all still be here solving problems no matter how this week goes.
Andrew Yang
Co-Chair, Forward Party
forwardparty.com (https://forwardparty.com/)
andrewyang.com (https://andrewyang.com/)

============================================================

You can ** update your preferences (https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/profile?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=de65787ec1&e=0f227c2ae3&c=29034e5e9e)
or ** unsubscribe (https://yangspeaks.us5.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=7448319cdb745ea2fd1b1b8a8&id=de65787ec1&t=b&e=0f227c2ae3&c=29034e5e9e)
.
clean_body: Hello, I hope that you are doing well as we process Election Day results. Hello, I hope that you are gearing up for Election Day. I voted early. What is going to happen on Tuesday? The first thing to know is that there’s a good chance we won’t know who won by the end of the night, so try to be patient. Who is going to win? What’s fascinating is that I’ve gotten reports of confidence from both sides. Both sides think that they’re going to win. The Republicans are in “who is going to take what job” mode. And Democrats who are looking at the early votes cast are confident that they’re going to pull it out, albeit narrowly. Their strategies are somewhat different. The Democrats are trying to get out their voters and leaning on field teams and organizers. It’s sensible given their monetary advantage. Republicans are reaching out to low propensity voters who are Trump-aligned but haven’t participated recently – hunters are one example. Trump won in 2016 largely by getting new voters to show up. You might think that well has been drained but the Republicans are testing it out again. “If the usual voters show up, the Democrats are confident they’re going to win. If new voters show up then Republicans think it’s going to be theirs,” is how one person put it. I was on CNN this week trying to prod the Harris campaign. I thought that Trump was going to win for quite a while – Kamala’s main path is to win Michigan, Wisconsin and Pennsylvania. The sunbelt states of Arizona, Nevada, Georgia and North Carolina seem to be slightly tougher terrain for her and better for Trump. I thought that passing up Joe Rogan was a missed opportunity, personally. It’s a massive audience that’s predominantly male – kind of a gold mine. The expectation is that the Republicans will win control of the Senate – they only need to pick up one seat aside from West Virginia – and the Democrats are slightly favored to win the House. In addition to the results that most people will be watching, I’ll of course be seeing how the Forward-endorsed candidates () fare as well as the ballot initiatives to open up the primaries across the country. This year I’ll be heading to Forward Party’s Election Night () event in Philadelphia – it should be a phenomenal gathering. Will it be festive or funereal? We’ll all find out soon enough. As always, do what you can. If you’d like to donate to Forward so we can have better choices in our politics, click here () . Try to keep a level head, we'll all still be here solving problems no matter how this week goes. Andrew Yang Co-Chair, Forward Party forwardparty.com () andrewyang.com () ============================================================ Want to change how you receive these emails? You can update your preferences () or unsubscribe () .

name: Mike Haridopolos
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MIKE HARIDOPOLOS FOR CONGRESS
body:
| | | | Dear Peter,
We’ve secured a landslide victory, and it’s all because of you.

Your support, your dedication, and your belief in this campaign carried us to victory, and I couldn’t be more grateful.

---
| [](https://link.support.mike4congress.)
---
I am honored to serve as the next Congressman for District 8. Thank you to the voters, to our volunteers, and to our supporters for your trust and confidence.

The voters spoke loud and clear in this election – they want a Congressman who will make America Affordable Again, stop illegal immigration, and get government spending under control.
I will work every day to do just that. And I’ll work to end partisan gridlock in Congress to get things done.
**At the link below you can watch our victory speech!**

[WATCH THE VICTORY SPEECH >>](https://link.support.mike4congress.)
The people of District 8 will always come first with me.
Now let's get to work!

With gratitude,
Mike Haridopolos Congressman-Elect for FL-8

PAID FOR BY MIKE HARIDOPOLOS FOR CONGRESS

|
---
| | [Unsubscribe.](https://link.support.mike4congress.com/click/e2751/VaHR0cHM6Ly9taG4ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/Sc3VwcG9ydF9taWtlRk9VUmNvbmdyZXNzX2NvbTI0MTEwNg/LbmNsS282SGxl/qP2hpZHM9bmN4ajZobDNVUVFscw/gZyvFKg/s4u9472274d)

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Last night’s victory was for you
body: ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=615225b3583e051d6f1f795d11244d7c11e90254a20c7428f23ba2c9cb09b9a352d56355d5ce2796cbc0e8596d07c391b9c944f466768d3e6e7d41a5d57a13db Peter, I’ve been having a hard time putting into words how grateful I am to have had your support in this election. I am truly so humbled and honored for all of the prayers and encouragement you’ve shown me over the course of this campaign. Last night’s victory belongs to the people of Tennessee and this great country. You have been heard. To everyone reading this who is struggling to make ends meet, to pay for gas and groceries; to the angel families who have lost loved ones from the violent criminals and rugs coming across our southern border, to all those who worry about sending your kids to play in your yard; to the parents who have worked with us to pass the Kids Online Safety Act; to the young and old who still believe America is the greatest country on earth – last night’s victory was for you. Just six years ago, Tennesseans elected me as the first woman Senator to serve the Volunteer State, and I am humbled that they chose to re-elect me to continue representing our conservative values in the U.S. Senate. I’m ready to work alongside the new Senate Republican Majority to pass the America First agenda under President Trump’s leadership. None of this would’ve been possible without you. Month after month you showed your commitment to this campaign. Your prayers, signatures, and donations made all the difference in the end. But I know I don’t say thanks often enough, so today I want to offer you a small token of my appreciation. My team recently re-ordered our best-selling Team Marsha merch, and we have a few left in stock. There’s only a limited quantity available, and we likely won’t re-order now that the election cycle is over. https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=615225b3583e051d6f1f795d11244d7c11e90254a20c7428f23ba2c9cb09b9a352d56355d5ce2796cbc0e8596d07c391b9c944f466768d3e6e7d41a5d57a13db So claim yours here! https://click.updates.marshablackburn.com/?qs=615225b3583e051d6f1f795d11244d7c11e90254a20c7428f23ba2c9cb09b9a352d56355d5ce2796cbc0e8596d07c391b9c944f466768d3e6e7d41a5d57a13db CLAIM MY GIFT Thank you again, Peter. I won’t ever forget your dedication to my fight, and your support will always mean the world. Sincerely, Senator Marsha Blackburn Paid for by Blackburn Tennessee Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Marsha for Senate, MARSHA PAC, and Tennessee Republican Party Federal Election Account. Don't miss this momentous occasion—let’s rally behind our President and celebrate! | Our hotel room block at the Forum Hotel by Kimpton is sold out, and we only have a few rooms remaining at our other locations! Click one below to book today, and make sure you buy your tickets at the same time! The Republican Party of Virginia PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF VIRGINIA

subject: Stop Your Taxes from Funding Abortions
body:
Every 80 seconds, a preborn child loses its life at the hands of Planned Parenthood

| | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.redconservatives.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_s=fa8a41ff986042088b5cfd94dca40d1e&_t=FrpyPoLgakdKtPkegKAy_h8yxg9w6VIV7EFGUc5dd9itj0TQD5o4IEfXKerdm-hpa-o7bWBrIk2SlBzso62ekHa-MHrICZrkQu14gbkpGJfkpwTD_yF2vjmmSGPCAoSX)_

#
Dear Friend,

Every 80 seconds, a preborn child loses its life at the hands of Planned Parenthood. This means that by the time you finish reading this email, another innocent life will be gone.

Despite the Supreme Court ruling in the overturning of _Roe v. Wade_ , Planned Parenthood continues to receive taxpayer funding, effectively subsidizing the intentional killing of preborn children. Since 2000, nearly $10.7 billion of taxpayers' money has funded their life-ending work.

Contrast this with the nearly 3,000 Pro-life pregnancy centers nationwide dedicated to supporting expectant mothers. Planned Parenthood only operates about 600 facilities and primarily offers one service: abortion. Alarmingly, they perform 62 abortions for every prenatal care service they provide, and prenatal care services are down 80% since 2010.

As a pro-life individual, I know you don't want your hard-earned tax dollars to support an organization that prioritizes abortion.

That's why I'm urging you to join us to demand an end to this taxpayer-funded ongoing tragedy. A new Congress and president will be chosen this November, and they need to hear from us _now_ that America does NOT support funding Planned Parenthood.

Make your voice heard. Sign our petition before midnight to demand an end to this grave misuse of your taxes. **[https://www.liveaction.org/defund-planned-parenthood-petition/](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=fa8a41ff986042088b5cfd94dca40d1e&_e=FM3Ihfg3HZnZVdFxJW7J5aF7EhViXT5j5xOcFsmBrMO6-bZiqSXIwOFb-gZyAt0bL_wv4Xc4HUHPt-maYlzk-R0zHp5hIJ_5c1-yWJFfjjmAOV7r4CNbBbdyAiN3U1qtsc9CJU6NX4hseKHyg6V7g_c5kYmTxBrmPc0xwf6nCQKZgLH3p9MRTFBGY0Xv7nU3O3UhPFj8YeHTlm9rNzoGZmjNtZuQzcbeAyskQVnt9oU69ZLKReg0m2aTa2kraH5khWK7d_bdYFwePeuRLG6p6ZwyAABT9KolzgkzDpi8tSNVwoQuSnKxDdhzOhqwgPu0XhJKexdVp_N9XcEC9WNhEbNrpRFeE5pa5xeRiTetUclut5-G5J5OWH12X18klIGxbsFXxTL6QhWEGwlYUZo8ZA%3D%3D)**

Thank you for standing with us for the protection of future generations!

For Life,

Lila Rose
**President and Founder**

[](http://links.redconservatives.com/u/click?_t=66ce1250519441959ebe70cf27686494&_m=fa8a41ff986042088b5cfd94dca40d1e&_e=FM3Ihfg3HZnZVdFxJW7J5aF7EhViXT5j5xOcFsmBrMO6-bZiqSXIwOFb-gZyAt0bL_wv4Xc4HUHPt-maYlzk-R0zHp5hIJ_5c1-yWJFfjjmAOV7r4CNbBbdyAiN3U1qtsc9CJU6NX4hseKHyg6V7g_c5kYmTxBrmPc0xwf6nCQKZgLH3p9MRTFBGY0Xv7nU3O3UhPFj8YeHTlm9rNzoGZmjNtZuQzcbeAyskQVnt9oXz0akfDsmd3MczI8qZpcBgUAOBudymPcEWeQXCZy-NwQGy-Y7GicTLzMFG4Zm6jGB1ITqwC75WFLL873JNyoiZTAMGYDKpS_ZK4XxqNZGJC9vu4SSnPrhSah5onVbTVr0JiiJxAGeHv8S-kaM4E_elFumhz8xVfiQEkxTEabXF4Q%3D%3D)

After you have had time to re-set your thinking and come to grips that the Blue Wave is not coming in 2024 after all, I'd like you to reflect on my thesis: maybe the data tools that the Dems have come to rely on are what are doing us in.
### **You 've probably heard of VAN**
If you've spent time canvassing, phone banking, or just hanging around at a democratic headquarters, you have heard the terms, "VAN", "Mini-VAN", "NationBuilder", "Vote Builder", or some variation on the above.
Since the first Obama run, when Hillary got out-teched by running a more traditional campaign against Obama's hip, slick, and cool, data-driven campaign, Democrats have knelt at the altar of screens, sort tools, and so-called "turf cuts". (For the uninitiated, a "turf cut" is a collection of names and addresses, assembled with a map, and handed to a volunteer for an optimized canvassing experience.)
### **My ride in the VAN**
I spent several weeks and several thousand dollars pursuing the dream that out there, somewhere, was a database that could be the key to winning my seat in New York's 21st Congressional District.
The real challenge is that there is no genuine competitor to NGP VAN. On the site, [Software Advice](https://substack.com/redirect/3f2f9d1f-b2a2-4cc8-b98d-300dcc33d1a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), an anonymous user writing in March 2024 said this about reasons for choosing NGP VAN: "There were no products we considered, there are not really serious competitors to the product." Anonymous had other remarks: "Very positive overall, it maintains all of the lists, data, call, knock, and text data as well as valuable targeting information...The interface continues to be clunky and somewhat difficult to navigate for new users. It functions first as a database as opposed to an active management tool.
I first encountered this world of Democratic data when I announced my candidacy in February 2024. I talked to a campaign data team that said I "must have" Nation Builder. I created a user account and entered my credit card details. I have no idea where there data came from or what was to be used for. Was this donor data? Door knocking data? Who knows? I exited the relationship (thankfully) with that data team.
The team that I hired to replace them was able to get me out of the Nation Builder contract. Unfortunately they also got me into a contract with NGP VAN that they told me was necessary for completing required financial reporting. Um. No. The version they got me contracted for doesn't do that. I needed another tool to do that. And in order to get that tool, I needed to spend another $1300 a month, with a contract until the end of the general election.
A user named Ace, writing in February 2024 made remarks that resonated with me. Specifically, Ace found that the "Help Desk was not helpful". And, as I found, the naming conventions of all of the various iterations of the platform are bewildering. Ultimately, this is what cost my campaign thousands of dollars. And no - once you sign a contract, your campaign is committed to regular billing until the end of the campaign cycle.
I was left with monthly invoices and no usable tools.
### Compare VAN to a county Board of Elections walking list
Indivisible made VAN data, or something substantially similar, available to all volunteers across the country. It was an amazing investment. A regular person could input their address, click, and find 10 voters they could go visit.
The weird thing was this: I could do that using the county Board of Elections lists, and they found 10 voters on my street or the next street over whom I could visit. The VAN list through Indivisible had me driving over 10 miles from my house.
### **Rural Districts**
Also on Software Advice, I found that Shelley, in August 2022, summed up the pros and cons of VAN the best: "PROS: As a tool it is far more than any rural county party could do for themselves, so it needs to be used to its fullest (but isn't). CONS:...but when used exclusively by volunteers, we can't get the continuity going. Without everyone using it, the input is sporadic and incomplete. There needs to be better coaching from the state party in how a rural county party can and should use VAN, with regular projects designed FOR RURAL COUNTIES (with county party input on the projects)....then deadlines and followup. Tying the why we do something to the results and analysis of what we learn each go-around (and how to track progress from election cycle to election cycle...in our RED county)."
Thanks, Shelley.
### **The cost of VAN**
I am a Democratic Candidate in a state that everyone thinks is a blue, until they look at a map.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/59ecb44b-07a0-4126-9034-e7fddedd782f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
While it is true, Kamala Harris won New York State, it's the urban centers that brought in the numbers. Only 16 of the state's 66 counties voted blue. The remaining 50 counties, including the entirety of my congressional district, voted red.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/412b29a2-7ced-42a0-bdeb-9756f9764728?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Consider that my congressional district, NY-21, spans 15 counties and over 15,000 square miles, taking up an enormous amount of land mass. The district encompasses all 6 million acres of the Adirondack Park, which in itself is big enough to fit Yellowstone, Yosemite, the Grand Canyon, and Glacier National Parks in its boundaries. And that's not all of NY-21. It stretches from just north of Albany, all the way to Plattsburgh, along the northern border to the St. Lawrence Seaway, and down to the Mohawk Valley. We're talking huge. See the smaller black-and-white inset map to see how this district dominates New York's landscape.
I was eager to acquire any data I could to make my race more manageable. The consultants I hired insisted that the state Democratic Party would provide VAN data. In fact, the VAN Help Desk insisted that my state Democratic Party would provide VAN data. Friends and colleagues who had worked on campaigns in other states insisted that my state Democratic Party would provide VAN data.
After numerous inquiries on my state Democratic Party's website, along with phone calls, and email messages, scattered over a period of 3 weeks, I got my answer: the New York State Democratic Party would provide my enormous congressional campaign with VAN data after I input my credit card details and consented to a charge of $8,500. Thank you very much.
### **What did I do? **
I vacillated. I really wanted this data, but I did not want to lose $8,500 of campaign funds on software that I, in my limited exposure, could not figure out and could not access a reasonable help desk support system.
The real kicker was that I could not even get a demo of VAN in order to see if it would be useful.
I tried to go "up the ladder", as we say, to see if someone in the state party could either give me access or could even help me identify whether VAN was useful for my ginormous congressional district.
A number of my county chairs said that they don't use VAN. It doesn't work for the rural counties.
I moved on. I actually did not think of VAN for several weeks. But as we got into canvassing, and I put together my own walking lists to present to town chairs, many people said, "Oh - I can just cut a turf in VAN."
There it was again. VAN. Many of the towns and a few of the counties had VAN, but they didn't know or didn't communicate it to me until we were way into the canvassing process. By that point I had printed literally thousands of pages of voter lists to wrangle my own canvassing project. And of course, handing a volunteer a stack of paper and a pencil is not nearly as slick as asking them to download a MiniVAN App.
How many trees did I lose on all of those pages that I printed?
But back to VAN. Here it was, in more than a few of the towns and counties where I needed it. What was it? How could it help?
### **The real question is: Does it help? **
As I understand it, VAN will take a voter's history, and in some cases (though I can't get anyone to give me a straight answer on how or when) they will mix in some amount of consumer data to determine the likelihood of voting for Democratic candidates in the upcoming election.
Whoa. Stop right there. You're mixing some undisclosed amount of what you learned about me from my Amazon shopping with my voting history to create a profile about me? Where did you get that data? Did I consent to use of that data? To what extent is the consumer piece factored into the profile you create about me? And do all of the profiles in VAN factor consumer data into the scoring? And what about my basic right to privacy?
Nobody could answer these questions for me.
After I choked on the data that was factored into the voter profile, I asked about the number that was assigned. Someone could be given a number of 85. Or 97. Or 43. The higher the number, the more likely it was that they were going to vote for Kamala Harris and the other Democrats on the ballot.
Ultimately, a canvassing organizer was to find out where a volunteer wanted to door knock, and together they would decide to visit voters who had a profile or score within certain numbers; say, 80-90. Higher than that, you were somewhat wasting your time; those voters would vote for you anyway. Lower than something around 70-75, and you were also wasting your time, as they were an unlikely voter.
I was able to do some canvassing with VAN. The questions were clunky. They told me nothing about what the issues were. Instead the scripts that had been prepared for this particular turf wanted me to rank, 1 to 5, the voter's likelihood of voting for certain candidates.
In other words, the script was trying to find more data about the people on whose door I knocked.
### **But how and when do we reach those people with low VAN scores? Do we ever reach them?**
Where we miss the mark is that we aren't talking to those people with low VAN profiles.
We need to talk to the Independents, and to the Republicans. We need to find the people who need persuasion.
### And therein lies the biggest fault in the system.
### We simply are not reaching them through VAN.
I heard it over and over through this election cycle: "We hit 1500 doors today." "We phonebanked and called 3,000 voters."
Great. But did you really even scratch the surface of the persuadable voter?
## _**If you did use VAN to reach those low-scoring voters, please let me know. I want to hear your story. Did it work? I'm open to suggestions, but I only have until around August of 2026 to figure this out. **_
### **What will I do differently next time? **
I will have more presence in the hyper-local newspapers. Here's why: I had a person show up to a Q&A in Fulton County who happened to see my banner ad in the local paper. She decided to show up to my event and find out more about me. I had similar stories in Malone, NY. I knocked on doors in St. Lawrence County and the voters said they had seen my ad in North Country This Week. And I picked up a few attendees to my events in Glens Falls by running a last-minute banner ad in the digital version of The Post Star.
### **Focus Groups**
Ideally, we would have focus groups, like we see on the television networks before a debate. People from all sides of the political spectrum would convene, listen to information, and share their views.
Sounds great. It's really tough to do.
To pull it off, you have to be able to reach out to a voter, convince them to show up, and have them speak their truth. Easier said than done. The norm is that Democrats show up to hear Democrats, and Republicans show up to hear Republicans.
Issues-based focus groups might work but they are still super tricky to shape. There were a few info sessions on NY's Proposition 1 (the Equal Rights Amendment) in my district. Some of these got downright heated. And it was interesting to find out how people learned about it. For some, it was as random as seeing a note on a bulletin board at the library. And I'm not sure that anyone attended who was truly unpersuaded, or that anyone left feeling any different from the way they felt when they walked in the door. Bias runs deep.
### **I 'll make more door-to-door visits with no regard for voter registration.**
Here's a Washington University in St. Louis [map from 2020](https://substack.com/redirect/07229d95-67ed-4469-b1d3-cb198d36ce55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the red and blue divide.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e4191dde-4e34-41d8-8f54-05b87d4c80c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It will be interesting to see an updated map for 2024.
We are not going to add more blue to this map if we only talk to other blue voters.
The best voter data system will be one that helps groups of volunteers, such as town committees, share information as to which streets have been visited by canvassers, who actually answered and who got the "canvassing light" touch of simply leaving literature on the door knob. Ideally, we would make a couple of other visits to actually have a face-to-face conversation.
The value of the technology would be to track whether a house had been visited. Dare I say a Google Sheet could accomplish this?
### **What I learned when I went door-to-door without regard for party affiliation**
Since I had no real working data tools, I went door to door blindly. Literally, my team and I drove to a neighborhood, said, "this looks good," and got out and started knocking on doors.
What I learned was that, other than meeting voters who explicitly wanted to see me defeat my opponent, most voters (usually 6 out of 10) thought the economy was top of mind. Others were worried about their kids' schools (not really a congressional issue, but I can listen and hope to troubleshoot). Veterans were disturbed by my opponent's handling of the PACT Act, and Trump's call that vets were "suckers and losers."
In other words, I had real conversations.
I convinced a few voters. I should have used more data tools to be able to tell you exactly how many.
### **I may not have the answers, but I do know that what we are doing is not working. **
Our current ways of organizing field work on campaigns are not working. I mean -- isn't that why we are all hurting this morning?
It's not working.
Don't even get me started about fundraising; that's for another column.
What I do know is that the vast majority of my voters in this huge rural district have not seen a candidate in decades - if ever. It's prime territory for Democrats (or Republicans) for a canvassing team to go door-to-door and build relationships.
Have conversations. Ask people, "What's important to you this election?" Most people will give you an earful.
And then I need everyone on that canvassing team to have some easy tool in their phone (or on paper for those Adirondack areas where we don't have cell phone coverage, along with the commitment to transfer that information to whatever tech tool we have when they are back at home with wifi) to keep us all informed about who was visited and what their top-of-mind election issue was. (Sidebar: I heard of voters in Pennsylvania expressing fatigue because they had been visited 4-5 times by teams, or called an equal number of times. Really? At some point we are pushing voters away with our zeal. Update the data and move to the next door!)
SIDEBAR: More than one volunteer has expressed fear of retribution or violence. For that reason, they will not go door-to-door blindly. I get it. We have had some horrible incidents. I can tell you that I have had some of the most amazing conversations with voters by going door-to-door.
Finding out what their top-of-mind issue was can inform me, the candidate, of what I need to be talking about in the press and at voter events.
### VAN fails in that it creates silos of pre-selected voters. By the time we are all settled in for watch night parties, we are convinced that our candidate will win because we had such positive responses when door knocking. But the basic criteria on which those doors were selected indicated that this voter was already leaning towards choosing your candidate.
I don't want a political tech tool that rates my voter based on how they voted the last 2-3 cycles, and their consumer choices. Ew. Leave their privacy rights intact, please.
I just want a tool that will allow me and all of the people on my team to walk the blocks, knock on the doors, have the conversations, share some of the issues with the rest of us.
Should I just use a Google Doc?
_If we haven 't met yet, I am Paula Collins, the Democratic and Working Families Party Candidate for New York's 21st Congressional District. Your comments are invaluable to me. Please let me hear from you. Remember that you can always visit my website at www.paulacollinsforcongress.com or by sending me a text at 315-230-3920. _

And of course, handing a volunteer a stack of paper and a pencil is not nearly as slick as asking them to download a MiniVAN App. How many trees did I lose on all of those pages that I printed? But back to VAN. Here it was, in more than a few of the towns and counties where I needed it. What was it? How could it help? The real question is: Does it help? As I understand it, VAN will take a voter's history, and in some cases (though I can't get anyone to give me a straight answer on how or when) they will mix in some amount of consumer data to determine the likelihood of voting for Democratic candidates in the upcoming election. Whoa. Stop right there. You're mixing some undisclosed amount of what you learned about me from my Amazon shopping with my voting history to create a profile about me? Where did you get that data? Did I consent to use of that data? To what extent is the consumer piece factored into the profile you create about me? And do all of the profiles in VAN factor consumer data into the scoring? And what about my basic right to privacy? Nobody could answer these questions for me. After I choked on the data that was factored into the voter profile, I asked about the number that was assigned. Someone could be given a number of 85. Or 97. Or 43. The higher the number, the more likely it was that they were going to vote for Kamala Harris and the other Democrats on the ballot. Ultimately, a canvassing organizer was to find out where a volunteer wanted to door knock, and together they would decide to visit voters who had a profile or score within certain numbers; say, 80-90. Higher than that, you were somewhat wasting your time; those voters would vote for you anyway. Lower than something around 70-75, and you were also wasting your time, as they were an unlikely voter. I was able to do some canvassing with VAN. The questions were clunky. They told me nothing about what the issues were. Instead the scripts that had been prepared for this particular turf wanted me to rank, 1 to 5, the voter's likelihood of voting for certain candidates. In other words, the script was trying to find more data about the people on whose door I knocked. But how and when do we reach those people with low VAN scores? Do we ever reach them? Where we miss the mark is that we aren't talking to those people with low VAN profiles. We need to talk to the Independents, and to the Republicans. We need to find the people who need persuasion. And therein lies the biggest fault in the system. We simply are not reaching them through VAN. I heard it over and over through this election cycle: "We hit 1500 doors today." "We phonebanked and called 3,000 voters." Great. But did you really even scratch the surface of the persuadable voter? If you did use VAN to reach those low-scoring voters, please let me know. I want to hear your story. Did it work? I'm open to suggestions, but I only have until around August of 2026 to figure this out. What will I do differently next time? I will have more presence in the hyper-local newspapers. Here's why: I had a person show up to a Q&A in Fulton County who happened to see my banner ad in the local paper. She decided to show up to my event and find out more about me. I had similar stories in Malone, NY. I knocked on doors in St. Lawrence County and the voters said they had seen my ad in North Country This Week. And I picked up a few attendees to my events in Glens Falls by running a last-minute banner ad in the digital version of The Post Star. Focus Groups Ideally, we would have focus groups, like we see on the television networks before a debate. People from all sides of the political spectrum would convene, listen to information, and share their views. Sounds great. It's really tough to do. To pull it off, you have to be able to reach out to a voter, convince them to show up, and have them speak their truth. Easier said than done. The norm is that Democrats show up to hear Democrats, and Republicans show up to hear Republicans. Issues-based focus groups might work but they are still super tricky to shape. There were a few info sessions on NY's Proposition 1 (the Equal Rights Amendment) in my district. Some of these got downright heated. And it was interesting to find out how people learned about it. For some, it was as random as seeing a note on a bulletin board at the library. And I'm not sure that anyone attended who was truly unpersuaded, or that anyone left feeling any different from the way they felt when they walked in the door. Bias runs deep. I 'll make more door-to-door visits with no regard for voter registration. Here's a Washington University in St. Louis of the red and blue divide. | | ---|---|--- It will be interesting to see an updated map for 2024. We are not going to add more blue to this map if we only talk to other blue voters. The best voter data system will be one that helps groups of volunteers, such as town committees, share information as to which streets have been visited by canvassers, who actually answered and who got the "canvassing light" touch of simply leaving literature on the door knob. Ideally, we would make a couple of other visits to actually have a face-to-face conversation. The value of the technology would be to track whether a house had been visited. Dare I say a Google Sheet could accomplish this? What I learned when I went door-to-door without regard for party affiliation Since I had no real working data tools, I went door to door blindly. Literally, my team and I drove to a neighborhood, said, "this looks good," and got out and started knocking on doors. What I learned was that, other than meeting voters who explicitly wanted to see me defeat my opponent, most voters (usually 6 out of 10) thought the economy was top of mind. Others were worried about their kids' schools (not really a congressional issue, but I can listen and hope to troubleshoot). Veterans were disturbed by my opponent's handling of the PACT Act, and Trump's call that vets were "suckers and losers." In other words, I had real conversations. I convinced a few voters. I should have used more data tools to be able to tell you exactly how many. I may not have the answers, but I do know that what we are doing is not working. ** Our current ways of organizing field work on campaigns are not working. I mean -- isn't that why we are all hurting this morning? It's not working. Don't even get me started about fundraising; that's for another column. What I do know is that the vast majority of my voters in this huge rural district have not seen a candidate in decades - if ever. It's prime territory for Democrats (or Republicans) for a canvassing team to go door-to-door and build relationships. Have conversations. Ask people, "What's important to you this election?" Most people will give you an earful. And then I need everyone on that canvassing team to have some easy tool in their phone (or on paper for those Adirondack areas where we don't have cell phone coverage, along with the commitment to transfer that information to whatever tech tool we have when they are back at home with wifi) to keep us all informed about who was visited and what their top-of-mind election issue was. (Sidebar: I heard of voters in Pennsylvania expressing fatigue because they had been visited 4-5 times by teams, or called an equal number of times. Really? At some point we are pushing voters away with our zeal. Update the data and move to the next door!) SIDEBAR: More than one volunteer has expressed fear of retribution or violence. For that reason, they will not go door-to-door blindly. I get it. We have had some horrible incidents. I can tell you that I have had some of the most amazing conversations with voters by going door-to-door. Finding out what their top-of-mind issue was can inform me, the candidate, of what I need to be talking about in the press and at voter events. VAN fails in that it creates silos of pre-selected voters. By the time we are all settled in for watch night parties, we are convinced that our candidate will win because we had such positive responses when door knocking. But the basic criteria on which those doors were selected indicated that this voter was already leaning towards choosing your candidate. I don't want a political tech tool that rates my voter based on how they voted the last 2-3 cycles, and their consumer choices. Ew. Leave their privacy rights intact, please. I just want a tool that will allow me and all of the people on my team to walk the blocks, knock on the doors, have the conversations, share some of the issues with the rest of us. Should I just use a Google Doc? If we haven 't met yet, I am Paula Collins, the Democratic and Working Families Party Candidate for New York's 21st Congressional District. Your comments are invaluable to me. Please let me hear from you. Remember that you can always visit my website at www.paulacollinsforcongress.com or by sending me a text at 315-230-3920. | | | | | | | (C) 2024 Paula Collins 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

body:
A note from Fair Fight |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96694886/510870138/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20241106_f_PostElection_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTUzYzJjNS03NzljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-lY4ja3bDVLYTsoaZs37UGJNUDLoO6z72x8lbcTuqZ0=&emci=0b8bc0bf-779c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=b553c2c5-779c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=1037277)

| | Friends, Today we’re holding space for this team. This outcome is not what we wanted for our friends, family, and our pro-voter community. We know folks are working through what it means to see Donald Trump rise to power once more. As the final vote totals continue to roll in, we need to keep this in mind: it looks like some 60 million Americans — who are registered voters — opted not to cast a ballot this year. If we are to create a pro-freedom governing majority in America, this is something we need to examine and understand. Our vote is our voice, and we need to make that clear across party lines. It’s also critical to remember: across the country, there were big wins down the ballot, including state legislative races here in Georgia, and ballot initiative wins for reproductive freedom and other key issues. Those victories help prove what we can achieve when we organize. We went all out, knocking on doors, making phone calls, mobilizing voters, and I could not be more grateful. The fight for voting rights in the states and at the federal level will become central to protecting our freedom as anti-voting forces are emboldened. Fair Fight and this community will have important roles to play. Our work to protect and expand the freedom to vote is more important now than ever. Let me take one more moment to say, thank you, friends, for your incredible support of Fair Fight during this critical year. Thank you to everyone in our community for giving your time, energy, resources, and contributions. Now we take a beat, process, and then chart our path forward together. Lauren and The Fair Fight Team | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96694887/510870139/-1598577384?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20241106_f_PostElection_v1s2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNTUzYzJjNS03NzljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-lY4ja3bDVLYTsoaZs37UGJNUDLoO6z72x8lbcTuqZ0=)

name: Michael V. Lawler
subject: Thank you, thank you, thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lawler for Congress, Inc
body:
This is all for you, and because of you

| | | | [ ](https://links.lawler4congress.com/els/v2/LrDKSmdpywcA/M3ZXckZBWEgxbUQ0Y3FzREQxQmgwcXVGYnVwYTdxSkVBUVExSzRzQ0FURzRzSmNnY29jNGN1RXhyQjJtRGFOc2FSVXM5ek1tdHVzRTJNemNsUU5Yd3NpcXdDVkVaSGJ2USttQzArdUdRNUE9S0/)
---
| | Peter, Thank you, thank you, thank you! After last night's historic victory – there’s only one thing I can say – **THANK YOU**. I can’t thank each and every one of you enough for everything you’ve done to make this happen. _I talked a lot in this campaign about being bipartisan, and last night’s victory is evidence that people listened._ While I am and always will be a proud Republican...Let me be clear: I would not have won tonight without the support of Democrats and Independents, who crossed party lines and I won’t let them down. We’ll lower the cost of living, support our law enforcement, and ensure the safety of our communities. That’s what this victory is about — delivering results for the people of New York, the people of the Hudson Valley, and the people of America. Together, we made history, not only in this race but in elections across the United States of America. I’ve always believed that regardless of party, background, or identity — Republican or Democrat, Black, White, Hispanic, Asian, Citizen, Immigrant, Gay, Straight, Jewish, Christian, or Muslim — we all must find ways to come together. We must listen to each other, understand each other, and work to find common ground so that every family in our community has the chance to succeed. _That is exactly what I’ve done, and what I will continue to do in Congress._ Thank you for trusting me with your support. Together, we will continue to make the Hudson Valley and America a better, safer, and more prosperous place for everyone. God bless you all. Congressman Mike LawlerRepublican, NY-17

clean_body: This is all for you, and because of you | | | | | | Peter, Thank you, thank you, thank you! After last night's historic victory – there’s only one thing I can say – THANK YOU. I can’t thank each and every one of you enough for everything you’ve done to make this happen. I talked a lot in this campaign about being bipartisan, and last night’s victory is evidence that people listened. While I am and always will be a proud Republican...Let me be clear: I would not have won tonight without the support of Democrats and Independents, who crossed party lines and I won’t let them down. We’ll lower the cost of living, support our law enforcement, and ensure the safety of our communities. That’s what this victory is about — delivering results for the people of New York, the people of the Hudson Valley, and the people of America. Together, we made history, not only in this race but in elections across the United States of America. I’ve always believed that regardless of party, background, or identity — Republican or Democrat, Black, White, Hispanic, Asian, Citizen, Immigrant, Gay, Straight, Jewish, Christian, or Muslim — we all must find ways to come together. We must listen to each other, understand each other, and work to find common ground so that every family in our community has the chance to succeed. That is exactly what I’ve done, and what I will continue to do in Congress. Thank you for trusting me with your support. Together, we will continue to make the Hudson Valley and America a better, safer, and more prosperous place for everyone. God bless you all. Congressman Mike LawlerRepublican, NY-17 | | | | | Mike Lawler is a Republican running for reelection in New York’s 17th Congressional District. name: Jonathan Nez
subject: A message for my supporters:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jonathan Nez for Congress
body:
| Hi Peter, As I sit here typing this up, I can't help but think about the incredible journey we've shared together. I know it's hard to wait for the final results in such a tight race — and trust me, I'm feeling the anticipation just as much as you are. But what's most important now is that every single vote is counted accurately which is why our election officials are working diligently around-the-clock to ensure that everyone's voice is heard. No matter the outcome, I'm humbled by the support and kindness you've shown me. This campaign has always been about the voices of rural Arizonans. In order to respect every rural vote and every rural voice, we are waiting for votes to be counted. Thank you so much, Jonathan Nez

5,086 votes for me with 2.00% of the vote. That's incredible. Thank you so much for voting for me.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7298868b-7672-4b38-b475-0d0a017ff7ac?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
While it is not the outcome I desired, it is a starting point. Having never run for any political office before, I didn't know exactly how to run a campaign or what to expect to be honest.
Trying to find a way to break through was difficult, especially running as unaffiliated with a political party.
With the results from the Presidential election and the re-election of Donald Trump, the political winds are shifting toward a demand for a more efficient government that actually acts in the best interest of the people.
That is the Executive branch of the government.
Now the Legislative branch of the government need to be reformed. That's what I ran on. Representing the people and reducing the corruption that plagues Congress.
I'd like to congratulate Celeste Maloy on winning the race but I'd like to let her know that I'm coming for her again. This time as a Republican in the primary.
The idea of voting with the people and creating secure elections needs to be heard.
Follow me as I and my team create the VotingAct app and roll it out to the people. Once they get a taste of being able to directly participate in the process of creating laws, I believe the idea will spread like wildfire.
May God bless you all and may God bless our country.
Tyler Murset

subject: Last night didn’t go our way. We still made an impact.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by NextGen Climate Action Committee; [http://nextgenpac
body:
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695429/510872414/-1995327540?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS85OTMyMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MTQ1NzFiYS03OTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sIhPHI2I-eyuP6sgQyK1P-Q8cD_slBVHY0oNZaDiOaE=&emci=0e4a5c8f-789c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=614571ba-799c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=6787172)
---
This isn’t the email I thought I’d be sending you today, team.
Not only did Donald Trump retake the White House, but Republicans have officially taken the Senate.
I’m sad.
I’m angry.
**And I’m so proud of everything this team of grassroots supporters has accomplished.**
I’m reaching out to you today to say THANK YOU for _everything_ you’ve invested in the youth vote. This team showed up in an incredible way and, together, we empowered young voters across the nation to take part in the political process.
And while this outcome may be painful to a lot of us, **unlike candidate campaigns, the work you funded won’t go away.** The young voters we activated in this election have the tools and knowledge they need to make an impact in _every other election they participate in_.
_So where do we go from here?_
As we begin the work to fight from the ground up to preserve our rights and freedoms we will continue to grow and strengthen this grassroots movement. NextGen was founded to uplift young voters and fight for progress. **That work will continue.**
At times like these, we have a choice: To despair or to fight back. We invite you to fight back with us.
In solidarity, [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695430/510872415/-1995327540?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS85OTMyMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MTQ1NzFiYS03OTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sIhPHI2I-eyuP6sgQyK1P-Q8cD_slBVHY0oNZaDiOaE=&emci=0e4a5c8f-789c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=614571ba-799c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=6787172)Cristina Tzintzún Ramirez
President & Executive Director
NextGen PAC
|

clean_body: | This isn’t the email I thought I’d be sending you today, team. Not only did Donald Trump retake the White House, but Republicans have officially taken the Senate. I’m sad. I’m angry. And I’m so proud of everything this team of grassroots supporters has accomplished. I’m reaching out to you today to say THANK YOU for everything you’ve invested in the youth vote. This team showed up in an incredible way and, together, we empowered young voters across the nation to take part in the political process. And while this outcome may be painful to a lot of us, unlike candidate campaigns, the work you funded won’t go away. The young voters we activated in this election have the tools and knowledge they need to make an impact in every other election they participate in. So where do we go from here? As we begin the work to fight from the ground up to preserve our rights and freedoms we will continue to grow and strengthen this grassroots movement. NextGen was founded to uplift young voters and fight for progress. That work will continue. At times like these, we have a choice: To despair or to fight back. We invite you to fight back with us. In solidarity, Tzintzún Ramirez President & Executive Director NextGen PAC | Paid for by NextGen Climate Action Committee; ; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | = |

name: Rick Becker
subject: Thank you for your support!
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by End the Unfair Property Tax – Julie Googins, Treasurer](https://endpropertytax
body:
Although Measure #4 didn’t make it this time, the overwhelming message is clear: meaningful property tax relief is urgently needed in our state... | | | | | |
---
[](https://endpropertytax.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9d6930e918a0489c6f7d6bb50&id=878a3e3d0c&e=d842fb25cc)
| | | | | Peter,

Thank you for standing with us.

I want to personally thank our volunteers, donors, and supporters for the the incredible dedication and support you poured into the fight for Measure #4.

While the measure didn’t pass last night, our mission to end the burden of property taxes in North Dakota remains stronger than ever.

Thanks to each and every one of you, we raised awareness, sparked critical conversations, and inspired many North Dakotans to imagine a future free from property tax.

Although Measure #4 didn’t make it this time, the overwhelming message is clear: meaningful property tax relief is urgently needed in our state. Now, it’s up to our legislators to do the right thing.

**I am calling on the North Dakota legislature to take action**.

True reform means enacting property tax relief by having the state take over all K-12 education funding. It’s time to move beyond a flawed system that relies on subjective valuations and confusing mill levies — taxes that are crushing hard-working North Dakotans.

We deserve a transparent, fair funding approach that reduces the tax burden on property owners and keeps local resources strong. I am urging lawmakers to put North Dakota families and businesses first by committing to these reforms.

Your commitment to meaningful tax reform has laid the foundation for the continued push to eliminate the state’s unfair property tax system. We are deeply grateful for your support and will keep fighting until we achieve the relief North Dakotans deserve.

Thank you for believing in a better future for North Dakota.

Sincerely,
---
|
---
|
---
[](https://endpropertytax.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9d6930e918a0489c6f7d6bb50&id=073a101576&e=d842fb25cc)
| Rick Becker
Chairman
End the Unfair Property Tax

before its too late...

| | _November 6th, 2024_
---
|
---
| | , It’s important you download your concealed carry certificate before it's banned for good.

Due to increased pressure from lawmakers, a website offering you a certificate for America’s Top CCW Gun Permit **could be shut down at any moment.**

Which is why it’s important you download your **Concealed Carry Certificate ASAP** Christopher.

Wait even one more day and we won’t be able to guarantee this certificate will be available to download.

This certificate is great for gun owners because it takes advantage of a little-known (but 100% legal) loophole - **[allowing you to get a CCW certification from this website](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19003/6374453/402ef138eadfca51d66e9f65aa2416e4554928b4/28c4a804022fff7c1a3777a78ad53800c17da7e3). You don’t even have to leave your house to do it!**

This loophole ensures you encounter: Zero government red tape (and no exposing your private info to the Feds)... Zero lines, classroom time or books to read... Zero gun range time (you don’t even need to fire a gun to get this permit). **[It’s as simple as visiting this site and answering a few questions (they’re simple)](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19003/6374453/402ef138eadfca51d66e9f65aa2416e4554928b4/28c4a804022fff7c1a3777a78ad53800c17da7e3).**

Plus you can complete the entire certification process online.

Download it directly to your phone or computer in as little as 30 minutes.

**You don’t even need to own a gun to get this permit...** that’s why it’s named America’s #1 Concealed Carry Permit.

31 states have agreed to accept this permit so far. That’s because it’s simply the closest and easiest path to a semi-nationally recognized permit that’s available right now.

This permit isn’t just a basic concealed carry permit either. **It’s one of a kind... and is available to you No Matter what state you reside in.**

**Hurry and get it now.**

**[There are no guarantees this website](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19003/6374453/402ef138eadfca51d66e9f65aa2416e4554928b4/28c4a804022fff7c1a3777a78ad53800c17da7e3)** will be able to legally deliver this certificate to your email address after today!

name: Team Kennedy
subject: Trump Wins! Let’s Make America Healthy Again
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TEAM KENNEDY
body:
Kennedy is ready to bring the MAHA mission to life.

|
---
| | | | [**Kennedy is ready to bring the MAHA mission to life!**](https://www.mahanow.org/donate-em-maha?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_election_11_6_24_1&n=1)

---
|
---
[](https://www.mahanow.org/donate-em-maha?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_election_11_6_24_1&n=2)
---
[](https://www.mahanow.org/donate-em-maha?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_election_11_6_24_1&n=3)
[](https://www.mahanow.org/donate-em-maha?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_election_11_6_24_1&n=4)
---
|
---
DONALD J. TRUMP WINS!
Dear Peter,
What a night! We did it. President-elect Donald Trump is heading back to the White House and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is heading to D.C.
**Kennedy has _ignited_ the Make America Healthy Again movement with a powerful call for a healthier, freer America.** His vision sparked a movement that has captured the nation’s attention, bringing health and truth to the forefront of our national dialogue.
This unprecedented political comeback makes Trump one of the most pivotal figures in U.S. history — _and you played a huge role in this historic win._
Here are just a few of the highlights that you helped make possible:
* We built a grassroots army of nearly 2 million strong.
* We raised more than $57 million to grow this movement.
* We put health and food safety front and center, right where it belongs.
* We united Republicans, Independents, Democrats, Libertarians, and other political parties in a powerful coalition for change.

Kennedy’s endorsement of President Trump will be remembered as one of the most influential in American history. It put restoration of health, accountability and integrity at the forefront, to drain the swamp and end corporate capture of our agencies.
[Donate Now](https://www.mahanow.org/donate-em-maha?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_election_11_6_24_1&n=5)
**With Trump’s return to office,_Kennedy is ready to bring the Make America Healthy Again mission to life_.** America is grappling with an epidemic of chronic disease—obesity, diabetes, cancer, and mental illness—that affects millions and drains our economy each year.
Now, with Trump’s support, RFK Jr. will reform federal health agencies, eliminate toxic additives, and end Big Pharma’s stranglehold on our health system. Together, they will transform our food and farming system, and clean up toxins in our air, water, and soil, ensuring the health and future of our nation.
[**Your unwavering commitment has brought us this far.**](https://www.mahanow.org/donate-em-maha?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_election_11_6_24_1&n=6) America remains strong, a country filled with patriots who have not lost hope.
The light on that hill has not gone out. _Because of you, it shines brighter than ever_.
**Every effort, every call, every dollar given, every hour volunteered, every conversation, and every prayer—none of it was in vain.**
[Donate Now](https://www.mahanow.org/donate-em-maha?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_election_11_6_24_1&n=7)
Together, with Trump in the White House and Kennedy in D.C., we will raise the banner of liberty and health and fight for a brighter, healthier future for our nation.
With profound gratitude and unbreakable resolve,
Team Kennedy
[Mahanow.org](https://www.mahanow.org/r?u=y_k5EP27yTOsxSw3tqBFMOXUy1MXBZnG7PhKqYOpRo0&e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=post_election_11_6_24_1&n=8)

Dear Peter,

By now you know that Donald Trump has won the 2024 election.

I know this is not what we’d hoped for, but **we have been preparing for this exact outcome.**

Now, more than ever, we need each other. [**I hope that you'll join us tomorrow, November 7 at 1PM ET for a post-election briefing call**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695743/510873570/-1206405312?sourceid=1150022&utm_campaign=25emkge-1101aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=election&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMWViYWRhZS03YTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KwPpyu_hoA3rm9NikrXk1-d7kSVkp_9B6vjpB50_mus=&emci=56e8805b-c29b-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=e1ebadae-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=954237) where we will outline our next steps and share how we intend to continue fighting with everything we have.

With unwavering determination,

Nancy Northup
President & CEO

P.S. For the full story on what these results mean for reproductive rights and how we're responding, please [**read my official press release.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695744/510873571/-2095265374?sourceid=1150022&utm_campaign=25emkge-1101aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=election&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMWViYWRhZS03YTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KwPpyu_hoA3rm9NikrXk1-d7kSVkp_9B6vjpB50_mus=&emci=56e8805b-c29b-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=e1ebadae-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=954237) | [**DONATE NOW**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695745/510873572/2141984067?sourceid=1150022&utm_campaign=25emkge-1101aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=election&amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMWViYWRhZS03YTljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KwPpyu_hoA3rm9NikrXk1-d7kSVkp_9B6vjpB50_mus=&emci=56e8805b-c29b-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=e1ebadae-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=954237 "DONATE NOW")

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695786/510873700/1328794144?refcode=ea.549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ms=ea.549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU0OWU1YzQ5LTdhOWMtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=BCrMHk00zgitAPFSeUO-zgcutJI1ZP9cNCjP4JJUin0=&emci=495d50c0-729c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=4823105)

The election outcome isn’t what we’d hoped for, to say the least, and we’re all feeling shock, grief, and fear and trying to process what it means. I’m right there with you. But I’ll tell you this, as soon as I realized what was happening on election night, my first thought was, “Thank God we have JanePAC, and we must keep going.” I deeply believe this is the best strategy for building an alternate base of power, a firewall against what’s coming. I also want you to know: Our fight didn’t end when the election was called. JanePAC isn’t going anywhere. We’re ready to resist every attempt by Donald Trump and his cronies to undo the climate progress we’ve made, and we’ll reject any push to let fossil fuel interests dictate our future. We’re going to hold the line and continue building the path forward for climate justice. Despite today’s outcome, there’s a lot we accomplished in this election. Across the country, climate champions endorsed by JanePAC achieved critical wins in key down-ballot seats. Leaders like Ann Johnson Stewart in Minnesota and Rep. Justin J. Pearson in Tennessee have stepped up and won their seats. They, along with other climate champions who won last night in Texas, California, Florida, Pennsylvania, Arizona, New Mexico, Nebraska, Wisconsin, and Illinois, are ready to protect our communities, enact clean energy solutions, and push back against powerful polluters. These victories remind us there is strength in our movement and real impact. Not just protest, but power. I hope you’ll continue this fight with us because we need every voice, every action, and every ounce of resolve to keep moving forward. We will be in touch about specific actions you can take with us in the days and weeks ahead, but for now — take care of yourself. We must all stay strong, rested, and unified. With you in love & solidarity, Jane Fonda [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695787/510873701/1328794144?refcode=ea.549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ms=ea.549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU0OWU1YzQ5LTdhOWMtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=BCrMHk00zgitAPFSeUO-zgcutJI1ZP9cNCjP4JJUin0=&emci=495d50c0-729c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=4823105)
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695790/510873706/2105508926?refcode=ea.549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ms=ea.549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU0OWU1YzQ5LTdhOWMtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=BCrMHk00zgitAPFSeUO-zgcutJI1ZP9cNCjP4JJUin0=) | [**DONATE TODAY**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96695791/510873707/2105508926?refcode=ea.549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ms=ea.549e5c49-7a9c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU0OWU1YzQ5LTdhOWMtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=BCrMHk00zgitAPFSeUO-zgcutJI1ZP9cNCjP4JJUin0=)

# [Democrats did this to themselves, and the rest of us](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151286870&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxMjg2ODcwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzA5MjM4NjksImV4cCI6MTczMzUxNTg2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4PQ525oCRWhHxt5YiXt2x5HizwvMbIMIaKZNPlw56lw)
### They fucked around and found out
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| Nov 6
---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151286870&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxMjg2ODcwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTczMDkyMzg2OSwiZXhwIjoxNzMzNTE1ODY5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.aKxUcp_5yznF0KAXim9J88GkR0uNHQQGilL-r2NNd7U&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151286870&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxMjg2ODcwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzA5MjM4NjksImV4cCI6MTczMzUxNTg2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4PQ525oCRWhHxt5YiXt2x5HizwvMbIMIaKZNPlw56lw&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
