| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/democrats-did-this-to-themselves?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---
The presidential election that just concluded was a [farce](https://substack.com/redirect/2e7bc59f-377e-4ca9-8942-24f0bf51bc18?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the beginning. I'm grateful for all the grassroots activists who took time and pounded pavement to educate their neighbors.
But sadly, they were failed by the Democratic Party in too many ways to count.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/21509af1-4fae-41b6-a2c5-af74cf441ebc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
No one should be surprised at the outcome.
### Failures despite warnings
Make no mistake: this was not a Trump victory, so much as a loss by Democrats of an election that could easily have won. Their most glaring failures include:
1. choosing both Biden and Harris through [back room deals](https://substack.com/redirect/633c18dc-ed78-4ffa-8788-f12c6ca81d19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg);
2. blocking a contested primary, which led to nominating an insider from [a one-party (i.e., corrupt) city](https://substack.com/redirect/4bea8ff3-cc90-4d7f-b1d6-2e95da3828cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) rather than a candidate with broad support;
3. [smearing leftists](https://substack.com/redirect/6e682212-c70f-46ab-85cf-81b019547fe5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and the [candidates of color](https://substack.com/redirect/c68edbe4-ee76-4a99-82fc-6e625d16746d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who have offered real alternatives, exposing their hypocrisy and [chasing](https://substack.com/redirect/9db9ec4d-052b-4bcb-bc81-a8614dd56e2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) voters of color into the arms of the GOP;
4. [agreeing with Republicans](https://substack.com/redirect/ee81f645-92e3-4b43-b862-87e0f8469357?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on policy issues from immigration to government secrecy, ethics, foreign policy, and restrictions on grassroots dissent;
5. repeatedly [aligning](https://substack.com/redirect/afa2c279-33ca-498c-932c-e954c43e16d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) themselves with [Wall Street](https://substack.com/redirect/21d57487-ea68-430a-b38a-15695e143ab7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) instead of [working class ](https://substack.com/redirect/162ac017-d8d2-4033-8761-556ec04681dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)Americans struggling to make ends meet, and;
6. allowing Netanyahu to disregard international human rights while waging an [escalating and expanding genocide](https://substack.com/redirect/0ce726b6-94d6-43b0-b09e-e1133c5374d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across the Middle East that [alienated](https://substack.com/redirect/286d4b40-6174-4aa0-989d-3c1246d5569d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) domestic constituencies on which Democrats knew they would need to rely.

_Inform your friends by sharing this post!_
[ Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=151286870&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTUxMjg2ODcwLCJpYXQiOjE3MzA5MjM4NjksImV4cCI6MTczMzUxNTg2OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.4PQ525oCRWhHxt5YiXt2x5HizwvMbIMIaKZNPlw56lw)
Harris chose to essentially [align](https://substack.com/redirect/21b5f6ca-0ec0-446b-89cb-2bb023725a4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) her campaign with the traditional Republican Party, particularly by treating Liz Cheney as a surrogate, while relying on various acts of [political theater](https://substack.com/redirect/458e3610-8349-4dbd-8ac2-8d91fc81082d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to project what amounted to a [cultural project with little policy substance](https://substack.com/redirect/31f6e446-d8dd-4212-bfe4-df2cf6215cb7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
This was a profound betrayal of the strategy embraced by Barack Obama in 2008. He at least [ran on a critique of militarism](https://substack.com/redirect/c4eb95e7-0947-4365-a47e-f6832a6ffc55?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), even if he came to [embrace and embody](https://substack.com/redirect/66d73a6e-a3c7-4dae-9653-5ba1fb929538?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) it as a president. In Harris, Democrats found another mixed race candidate with a three syllable name, but one who offered not even the pandering message that propelled Obama to the White House.
No one should be surprised at the outcome.
### Can you hear me now?
I've been warning about the rise of fascism in the United States since [long before it became fashionable](https://substack.com/redirect/35378cfe-2044-4307-bd60-8f26cea3c89c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Oblivious to their own complicity, Democrats have spent decades setting the stage for this moment.
It brings me no joy to observe the overt adoption of fascism as a national ideology. But we need not lie to ourselves.
The rise of the right wing is a predictable result of the Center's failures to support ideas offered from the Left to support working Americans and their families. I frankly would prefer to have been wrong rather than prescient.
### Journalists did this, too
Beyond Democrats and the [fealty to capital](https://substack.com/redirect/9da47f54-cf37-4236-85bb-0cc0c0658c66?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that unites them, the community of [corporate journalists should also bear a great deal of the blame](https://substack.com/redirect/2da94d40-4e42-42e2-ad1f-9207cc370916?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for Trump's return to Washington. The [rise of propaganda](https://substack.com/redirect/4bea8ff3-cc90-4d7f-b1d6-2e95da3828cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and the loss of trustworthy news media has been a crucial part of the recipe on which Republicans relied.
_Chronicles of a Dying Empire is a publication supported by readers, rather than advertisers.**Subscriptions are free**. To support my work as a dissident dedicated to holding power accountable, **please consider becoming a paid subscriber**._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..YRm_iK1Zl8z2YxfJ4jun_Rco961J6L5ui15APvY7BjE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=151286870)
Time and again, journalists have served as propagandists working to justify horrors in plain sight emanating from the bipartisan cesspool in Washington. Yet the grassroots outrage suppressed by institutional voices unwilling to hear it has, for better or worse, now forced itself to the fore.
I'll leave anonymous one Trump voter with whom I've spoken, but want to share his perspective.
He's a university professor in a blue state agonized by the Democratic Party's longstanding corruption, as well as the media's casual deference to it. He perceives in Trump a voice willing to defy business as usual. We discussed the professor's policy preferences, and how little Trump could be trusted to pursue them, or even match any of his own rhetoric with action.
This voter responded by observing Trump's [invitation](https://substack.com/redirect/ff27f360-d83a-483d-844b-d4ff847a5690?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of RFK to join his campaign, describing that decision as proof of the iconoclasm that, to him, was more important than any particular policy. Harris had the same opportunity--which alone could likely have ensured her victory--but [squandered it](https://substack.com/redirect/548547cb-72a8-4b23-996f-5b535822463e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Voters have clamored for change for decades, only to be rebuffed by a bipartisan establishment more attuned to culture, appearances, war, and Wall Street than to the needs of working families increasingly struggling to survive.
Their repudiation of Harris & her [legions](https://substack.com/redirect/072a722b-87b8-430a-8d8c-da2fa535230c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of [celebrity](https://substack.com/redirect/4a1ca1d1-3b67-40c6-9b40-54fbc1212435?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) endorsers represents a populist rejection of elites, dominant institutions, and the public narratives constructed by newspaper editors--to at least the same degree as it might be ([fairly](https://substack.com/redirect/4f1fdfe6-689b-45fc-9f11-a20b551f5181?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) described as conveying any substantive mandate supporting either [misogyny](https://substack.com/redirect/87077508-fbfc-4952-92b2-996f7911194e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or white supremacy.
### Thank YOU for overcoming at least one hurdle
To the extent readers need a hopeful coda to process what might be a painful moment, I would just applaud anyone--including you--for participating in and supporting the independent media ecosystem.
Our country's most [informed](https://substack.com/@chrishedges) and [insightful](https://substack.com/@stephensemler) voices are routinely [suppressed](https://substack.com/@rupamarya/p-150249834) in this country in favor of narratives that support, reify, and enable capital and power. By stepping outside the traditional media ecosystem and curating your own information sources, you're taking an active and important step towards cultivating the independent insight that no one in Washington wants you to wield.
I hope to continue offering whatever insight I can going forward. Thanks for making it possible for me to continue speaking out!
#### Invite your friends and earn rewards
If you enjoy Chronicles of a Dying Empire, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe.

name: Heather Williams (via DLCC.org)
subject: Here’s what I can share for now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee, dlcc
body:
My team and I have had a long night awaiting election results. As we wait for more information to drop, I want to share what we know. | | |
---
| |
---|---
It’s DLCC President Heather Williams. It is no secret that last night wasn’t the night we had wanted. I, like all of you, had hoped we would wake up to different results. What’s ahead of us as a country feels heavy and scary. But in electoral politics, it is the voters who make the ultimate decision. We must accept their choice and do our work in this new context. _In a night of hardship, I want to offer you a few bright spots._ Last night, North Carolina Democrats won enough seats to break a Republican supermajority and hand veto power to Governor-elect Josh Stein. In Wisconsin, we’ve gained at least 10 seats in the Assembly and four seats in the Senate, putting the Wisconsin Senate in play for 2026 – the first time in 16 years. And in Minnesota, we defended our Senate majority in a crucial special election. This work has never been easy, especially in moments like this, but know that we are making sure the outstanding ballots are counted as we face uncertainty in many chambers. As of 2 p.m. ET, nearly 600 state legislative races have yet to be called – 150 of those are in our battleground states – that we’ll continue to monitor in the coming days. What is also true is that elections are about more than who wins or loses on Election Day – they’re about the future we want for ourselves, the people we care deeply about, and our communities all across this country. I want to say thank you again to you and everyone across the country who made phone calls, knocked on doors, and donated to support our mission. Our movement may have faced a setback, but we’ll come back stronger ready to pick up the fight. More soon, Heather Heather Williams
President
Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee

|
|
---
Paid for by the Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee, dlcc.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

Contributions or gifts to DLCC are not tax deductible. | The Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee is dedicated to winning and defending Democratic majorities in state legislatures across the country. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of our grassroots network.

Emails are a crucial way to stay in touch with our supporters. Please add us to your address book or safe senders list to ensure timely election updates.

We're fighting to elect Democrats and advance a progressive vision in the states. Our success depends on grassroots donors like you. Ninety-eight percent of our donors give less than $200, and two-thirds give less than $20. clean_body: My team and I have had a long night awaiting election results. As we wait for more information to drop, I want to share what we know. | | | | | ---|--- It’s DLCC President Heather Williams. It is no secret that last night wasn’t the night we had wanted. I, like all of you, had hoped we would wake up to different results. What’s ahead of us as a country feels heavy and scary. But in electoral politics, it is the voters who make the ultimate decision. We must accept their choice and do our work in this new context. In a night of hardship, I want to offer you a few bright spots. Last night, North Carolina Democrats won enough seats to break a Republican supermajority and hand veto power to Governor-elect Josh Stein. In Wisconsin, we’ve gained at least 10 seats in the Assembly and four seats in the Senate, putting the Wisconsin Senate in play for 2026 – the first time in 16 years. And in Minnesota, we defended our Senate majority in a crucial special election. This work has never been easy, especially in moments like this, but know that we are making sure the outstanding ballots are counted as we face uncertainty in many chambers. As of 2 p.m. ET, nearly 600 state legislative races have yet to be called – 150 of those are in our battleground states – that we’ll continue to monitor in the coming days. What is also true is that elections are about more than who wins or loses on Election Day – they’re about the future we want for ourselves, the people we care deeply about, and our communities all across this country. I want to say thank you again to you and everyone across the country who made phone calls, knocked on doors, and donated to support our mission. Our movement may have faced a setback, but we’ll come back stronger ready to pick up the fight. More soon, Heather Heather Williams President Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee | | Paid for by the Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee, dlcc.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to DLCC are not tax deductible. | The Democratic Legislative Campaign Committee is dedicated to winning and defending Democratic majorities in state legislatures across the country. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: How we respond
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
I will not ask you to look for silver linings

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2411%2E4032634%2ETEzLHY)
---
I will not ask you to look for silver linings. I will not ask you to feel anything but grief right now. The consequences of this election will be real and devastating. But I’m reminding myself that on the road ahead, there will still be opportunities to fight back. I can’t tell you we will win all of those fights. I can’t tell you we will win most or even any of them. But when we arrive at each of those moments, we will face a choice: to give up, or to press forward. We can’t control everything that comes next. But we can control how we respond. The far right wants us to feel powerless. Extremists are counting on apathy, cynicism, heartbreak, or all the above to be their rocketfuel. They are counting on us to point fingers at each other and lose trust in our ability to ever, ever make change. I absolutely refuse to give them the satisfaction. Feeling powerless is the first step toward becoming powerless. By staying united, by refusing to surrender, we give ourselves a fighting chance. We will continue to fight for each other. Eight years ago, in the dark days when Republicans took full power in Washington, I thought the Affordable Care Act would be gone with a snap of a finger. But the American people rose up. Activists like the late, inimitable Ady Barkan and countless others put their bodies on the line. They made their voices heard. They saved health care for millions of families. And a grassroots movement against far-right control took back the House in 2018, the White House in 2020, and the Senate in 2021. Don’t let anyone tell you that those victories didn’t make a real difference in people’s lives. More people could afford to go to the doctor and fill a prescription. More people could go to work. More parents could afford to put food on the table for their kids and buy new coats for them in the winter. Lives were saved. And as the far right works to roll back what we’ve achieved, they’ll hope we won’t have the stomach to push back. But we can choose to prove them wrong. We need each other. We take care of each other. And please, take care of yourself. Take the time you need right now to be angry, hurt, and confused. Hold your loved ones close. Find opportunities to be in community with others this week. Take social media breaks. Foster your connections. Make new ones. Reach out to someone you’ve lost touch with. Tell them you’re with them no matter what lies ahead. If the work you do makes a difference for just one person, that would be enough. I will wake up every morning and choose to fight for our families, our freedoms, and our kids’ futures. I will do everything I can in my position to defend our values and fight back. And I will always be honored to fight by your side. [**In the immediate-term, my colleague Jacky Rosen’s Senate race in Nevada is still too close to call right now. We need to do everything we can to ensure Republicans can’t gain any more power in the Senate. Every vote will matter. If you can pitch in anything you can to her campaign, your full donation will help support their ballot curing operation.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/2816?t=8&akid=2411%2E4032634%2ETEzLHY) \- Elizabeth P.S. To everyone who volunteered, who phone banked, who texted, who knocked doors, who brought cookies to the volunteers, who put up a lawn sign, who gave part of themselves to be part of something bigger — for our re-election campaign in Massachusetts and for campaigns across the country — thank you. You are the beating heart of our democracy.

---
| | | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/365?t=4&akid=2411%2E4032634%2ETEzLHY) | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/367?t=5&akid=2411%2E4032634%2ETEzLHY) | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/368?t=6&akid=2411%2E4032634%2ETEzLHY) | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/369?t=7&akid=2411%2E4032634%2ETEzLHY)

clean_body: | | | What did I tell ya, Peter!? For months, I’ve been saying that Jacky Rosen’s race in Nevada is going to come down to the smallest of margins. Now, here we are the day after the election, and the race is still TOO CLOSE TO CALL. So I need you to listen up: Jacky had to spend every last dollar she had to fight back against the $75 MILLION that the GOP dumped into this race to buy this seat. Today, she needs urgent grassroots support to prepare for what’s next. So I’m pleading with you to show your support for Jacky’s race at this pivotal moment: If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: Thank you for helping us take this all the way to the end! – James Paid for by Defeat Boebert PAC

name: Evangelicals for Harris
subject: EFH: You Are Needed
body:
Keep Doing Good | |
---
| | | If there is one thing I know from the results of this election it is this: we are needed now more than ever; **you are needed now more than ever**.

Our Evangelicals for Harris efforts have never been about partisanship. Vice President Harris and many other democratic candidates lost. **But our fight to create a better world goes on — and it just got harder. That’s why we’re needed now more than ever.**

This is a hard loss and the future seems grim. What are our feelings right now — grief, despair, fear, apathy? Or maybe we are numb?

**The antidote is the same: action to create the common good. **

Nix the blame-game and the doom scrolling and **let’s do something.**

**In the near term it doesn’t even need to be something earth shattering, it can be rather commonplace. **Tackle some home projects. Do a deep house cleaning. A hallmark of our campaign has been to encourage our folks to perform acts of service in their neighborhoods or communities. **What you want is to be able to see the positive results when you are finished.**

**_Remember that we are in this together. By definition, a single person cannot create the common good. If you see others struggling, if they have become disengaged, invite them to join you in your actions_** _._

For the longer term, stay connected to groups that are doing things to make a difference. As I said, **because of this election there will be even more work for us to do**.

**Finally, as is always the case, _people of action need to be centered in prayer to remind us that we are never alone._ _God is always with us._ **

**As Jesus said, _“And behold, I am with you always, to the end of the age”_ (Mt. 28:20)**

Thank you for all you’re doing. We’re praying for you.

The Rev. Jim Ball, Ph.D.
Founder and Chair
Evangelicals For Harris

name: Dan Crenshaw
subject: Thank You
body:
| | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JC1K7QETPT1S6ECER63W4PSG_0)
---
|
---
| Peter, I want to express my sincere gratitude for everything you’ve done to help us achieve victory last night.

Your donations, your support, and your votes have quite literally changed the course of American history.

Because of you, I won a landslide victory last night; Donald Trump is returning to the White House; the Senate has flipped back to Republicans; and, pending a few races that are too close to call, Republicans will hold onto the House.

As long as Republicans remain united over the next two years, there’s nothing we can’t accomplish for this great nation.

Thank you from the bottom of my heart. I’m blessed to have you on my side.
---
| In Service,

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: We need to make sure every last vote gets counted
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Nevada State Democratic Party, NVDEMS
body:
This race, and its stakes, are higher than ever. | | | |
---
Here's where things stand, friend: **Nevada’s Senate race is still too close to call – which means our work isn’t done yet. And we’re expecting my race to come down to a handful of votes.**
We cannot afford to lose a single additional Senate seat. If Republicans flip Nevada red, they’ll have even more power to plow through with their destructive agenda – including passing a nationwide abortion ban.
Winning is going to require ensuring that every last vote gets properly counted. So today, I need to make the most important ask I've ever made of you: [**_Please, will you split $20 now – or whatever you can spare – between my campaign and NV Dems to show the huge wave of support we need to keep Nevada blue?_**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtEEjhuOfSZtU0xhsJqEpvPCvEPhzQ3eFVNrKyq9t1o0LNZs2v33T2luyDVS3heY8R8Xhj0BiJz9bRi5ng6_G7CogjUDXQ3frgzTK0npjKhDkCI5WxBW4DAUpr7-LU0yzjiDVPkhsAxvrW4u0kaDPrtM2UwPdCW9F58OnSzx-sODU2gOcTYv3L1HG4rzSkEhNMJnK87aa5Euc5wJYC7pXZ4yxXCceJBYvZe2N67GW-eisSKWOqjFhIW8mJr_2cSwT0MyY34qgHUr9ZcNZkcjNL9u7fy6_x-MdJWN16_7uHsjzO2qy2LdU7iBqQImk77bvyXjIvQTzduLbzZemYRawRp-m7KZucp1SxB_4UvUVZjC4TUIj2APXIKjhUEsyTahfa9O7WwaaydLY576rEDnU6inmxkFUWOcWuXB5oZkZwSVFRbfBFW3Req6yXcFyIsPWE/4b8/8dhdt-R9QqGOgNsCfEKGKQ/h0/h001.E-11d4xKFtRumQ23hCPYvSVanUivERLt7SE9jcVp2_Q)

We laid it all on the line to counter their attacks and turn Nevada blue. In order to do that, we had to spend every last dollar we had just to get through Election Day – because our race was one of the most expensive in the whole country.
Our work isn’t done until every vote in Nevada is counted. But today, we’re starting all over again from scratch, which is why we’ve set an emergency goal to raise $500,000. And now, the stakes are higher than ever – we absolutely need to reach this goal to win.

clean_body: This race, and its stakes, are higher than ever. | | | | Here's where things stand, friend: Nevada’s Senate race is still too close to call – which means our work isn’t done yet. And we’re expecting my race to come down to a handful of votes. We cannot afford to lose a single additional Senate seat. If Republicans flip Nevada red, they’ll have even more power to plow through with their destructive agenda – including passing a nationwide abortion ban. Winning is going to require ensuring that every last vote gets properly counted. So today, I need to make the most important ask I've ever made of you: We laid it all on the line to counter their attacks and turn Nevada blue. In order to do that, we had to spend every last dollar we had just to get through Election Day – because our race was one of the most expensive in the whole country. Our work isn’t done until every vote in Nevada is counted. But today, we’re starting all over again from scratch, which is why we’ve set an emergency goal to raise $500,000. And now, the stakes are higher than ever – we absolutely need to reach this goal to win. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | Thank you so much. Jacky The Nevada State Democratic Party fights for progress in the Silver State. We believe that we are stronger together than on our own, and that America succeeds when everyone gets a fair shot, everyone does their fair share, and everyone plays by the same rules. | | Paid for by the Nevada State Democratic Party, NVDEMS.com, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Ruben Gallego
subject: You have an important role to play right now, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lucas Kunce for Missouri
body:
I’m confident that — after all the votes are counted — we’re going to win. But it’s going to take a little while until that happens. And I can tell you this, for sure: Whatever the result is, it’s going to be close.
Peter —
When I first launched this campaign, I never would’ve dreamed that this many people would have my back.
It’s unbelievable, and I’ll always be grateful for everything we built together.
Here’s where we’re at in Arizona:
I’m confident that — after all the votes are counted — we’re going to win. But it’s going to take a little while until that happens. And I can tell you this, for sure: Whatever the result is, it’s going to be close.
Unfortunately, my opponent has a history of fanning the flames of conspiracy and disinformation. She’s already done a ton of damage to my state and to our country, but I’m not about to back down.
Maybe it’s the Marine in me, but I’ll always fight for our democracy. And you have a critical role in that fight, if you’re up for it.
**[If you’re looking for some way to have an impact and help us fight back against Kari Lake as we work to ensure the votes are counted, please make a split donation to my campaign and Lucas Kunce.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/83918?refcode=em20241106-8651&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8651%2E614464%2E03ifvP)**

Team,

First, I need to thank you again for supporting this team. We wouldn’t have made it through Election Day without you and your incredible support!

I wanted to give you an update on where things stand right now: There are still thousands of outstanding ballots and, as of right now, our race is too close to call.

We knew this race was going to be close, and that’s a testament to our strength as a team.

**We’ll keep you updated as things continue to unfold but we’re confident that, once every vote is counted, we’ll deliver a win for Oregon.**

Thank you again. We’re in this together!

Janelle

---

| Janelle Bynum is running for in Oregon's 5th Congressional District. name: Debbie Mucarsel-Powell
subject: It’s been the greatest honor of my life
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Debbie for Florida
body:
Friend,
The results are in, and while it’s not what we wanted, I am so proud of the work we’ve done since we launched our Senate campaign over a year ago.
We couldn’t have gotten this far without your support and hard work, friend.
Together, we reached out to voters all around the state and traveled millions of miles. And right now, I’m thinking about all of the Floridians I met along the way who felt that same sense of importance to do right by our country:
The woman in Pinellas who was a Republican, but voted for me because she was tired of the hate and wanted to move forward.
The small-business owner in Fort Myers Beach who was fighting for climate action and lower insurance rates after his store faced hurricane after hurricane.
The first-time voter in Miami who cast her first ballot for me after becoming an American citizen this summer.
The college student in Gainesville who was supporting me in his first election even though his entire family was voting Republican.
And the women and men all around Florida who are in the fight for reproductive freedom.
I’m honored to have been the first Latina Democratic nominee for Senate in Florida.
And I know that these losses are hard, friend. But we cannot give up that sense of hope and joy that pushed us throughout this campaign. I know that we moved the needle, and while it may not be our time this year, we must refuse to give up in the fight for our rights and freedoms.
I know that every Floridian involved in this fight will continue to say no más to the hatred and division, and will hold Scott and Trump accountable at every turn. The fight for our freedoms doesn’t end today — it is just beginning.
It has been the joy of my life to fight for you and your family.
Thank you,
Debbie

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Team,
Today is a tough day. We’ve lost the White House, and our Senate majority is gone. We know many of you are feeling the weight of this, just as we are.
This setback hits hard — after all we’ve fought for, and all the progress we’ve made. Right now, more than anything, we want to hear from you. **We’re asking you to take a moment and hit reply to share what’s on your mind**. While we can’t respond to every message, know that our team will read each one.
_What worries you most about this shift? What gives you hope for the road ahead?_
We’re in this together, and we’ll keep fighting side by side. Today, though, we just want to listen.
Thank you for standing with us, always.
– Upset the Setup Team

(www.upsetsetup.org)

name: Scott Fitzgerald
subject: We did it! Thank you, Peter.
body:
| | | Peter, Election Day is over, and I want to say thank you, from the bottom of my heart, for supporting my campaign. Whether with your vote, time, or donation, your support has not gone unnoticed. I've said it before, but winning is just the first step; **now it's time to lead.** **With President Trump in office, this is a perfect opportunity to restore common sense and get our country back on track. ** America is facing a lot of challenges, but I am confident we can find solutions. I look forward to continuing to serve you and getting back to work to secure our border and bring down inflation, gas prices, and crime rates. Thank you for everything, **Scott Fitzgerald** _U.S. Congressman for Wisconsin's 5th District_

name: Chrissy Houlahan
subject: Little Lucy and the Purple Crayon
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96697352/510879153/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjJjY2UxNi04MjljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k8W4yvwh7lVrvYL8KLPTPwGA6VSWVj7wFvqZQ48maLw=&emci=af1bf428-689c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=222cce16-829c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=21278485)
---
Derek –
Just weeks before the election, little Lucy joined the world 5 weeks early and weighing in at less than 5 pounds.
She is my first grandchild – a granddaughter. Lucy was named after my great grandmother – a fierce warrior of a woman responsible for saving my young father and his mother from the Holocaust that murdered millions of Jews. Lucy means light. And little Lucy is mine.
Little Lucy celebrated her first-ever Halloween in the NICU last week. My daughter and her wife dressed Lucy up in a pale blue onesie and crafted her a huge purple crayon. They posed with her as “Lucy and the Purple Crayon” in a nod to the children’s storybook _Harold and the Purple Crayon_. This is a story of a little boy who literally draws the world he wants to see as he navigates through the night.
The election last night broke my heart and head. I’m certain there are many of you out there who feel similarly dizzied by the results. I know how hard so many of us in Pennsylvania and the rest of the country worked to help get Kamala Harris elected so that she might be able to continue working on all of our behalf on the progress and promise of our nation. I know how much it meant to us all.
I know how stung and confused you are. Please know you’re not alone. And please also know this is not the end of our journey and fight. The arc of the universe is long.
We will continue to work to advance the common agenda of this great and generous nation because it’s universal, important, and just. And we will do this knowing that there will be setbacks and huge disappointments such as this.
But we will do this for little Lucy and the light that she and her generation bring into this world. We will do this so that she and others who are just now born and who are not yet born will be able to draw their worlds.
So, allow yourselves the time to grieve and to question. And take care of yourself and those around you who are doubtless hurting now. But then, get up, brush yourself off, pick up your crayon, and get back at it. We need you. Lucy needs you. We have a world to draw.
– Chrissy
| Chrissy Houlahan for Congress
P.O. Box 222
Devon, PA 19333

name: Matt Cartwright
subject: The greatest honor of my life.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cartwright for Congress
body:
I'm proud of the race we ran.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/96697400/510879294/1790176412?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzgyNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxZDJjY2UxNi04MjljLWVmMTEtODhjZS0wMDBkM2E5OGZhNmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=B2oAsMThDCEUbC-CW82KSDrJC4upRHQ5EDF2Pn7W5do=&emci=0fa182b7-679c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=1d2cce16-829c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=23311593) | Peter –

Last night, I congratulated Rob Bresnahan on his win and officially conceded in the race to represent Pennsylvania’s Eighth Congressional District.

While this isn’t the outcome we’d hoped for, I want you to know that my heart is full of gratitude and pride for all that we’ve accomplished together. I am beyond grateful for your hard work, your commitment, and your belief in what we can achieve together.

To each of you who volunteered, donated, showed up, and put in the hard work – you gave everything for this campaign. You made calls, knocked on doors, and shared our vision with your neighbors, friends, and family. In a race this close, your voices were heard, and your commitment was felt across our community. I cannot thank you enough.

**Representing northeastern Pennsylvania has been the greatest honor of my life.** I am immensely proud of the progress we’ve made together. Whether it was securing federal investments for our local law enforcement, improving our infrastructure, or supporting economic development, I fought every day to bring meaningful change back home – and I did so with immense bipartisan support.

Thank you for standing with me – _and know that even as my term comes to an end, I will always be standing with you._

With gratitude and pride,

Matt Cartwright

clean_body: I'm proud of the race we ran. | Peter – Last night, I congratulated Rob Bresnahan on his win and officially conceded in the race to represent Pennsylvania’s Eighth Congressional District. While this isn’t the outcome we’d hoped for, I want you to know that my heart is full of gratitude and pride for all that we’ve accomplished together. I am beyond grateful for your hard work, your commitment, and your belief in what we can achieve together. To each of you who volunteered, donated, showed up, and put in the hard work – you gave everything for this campaign. You made calls, knocked on doors, and shared our vision with your neighbors, friends, and family. In a race this close, your voices were heard, and your commitment was felt across our community. I cannot thank you enough. Representing northeastern Pennsylvania has been the greatest honor of my life. I am immensely proud of the progress we’ve made together. Whether it was securing federal investments for our local law enforcement, improving our infrastructure, or supporting economic development, I fought every day to bring meaningful change back home – and I did so with immense bipartisan support. Thank you for standing with me – and know that even as my term comes to an end, I will always be standing with you. With gratitude and pride, Matt Cartwright | "Facebook" "Twitter" "Twitter" | Paid for by Cartwright for Congress | Cartwright For Congress PO Box 414 Scranton, PA 18501 United States Click here to from the Cartwright for Congress.

Hey there, Peter.

While this is not the outcome we hoped for, I just wanted to reach out and say thank you for supporting my campaign.

I’m incredibly proud of what we accomplished in South Jersey. Whether you donated, talked to friends and family to get them on board, or put up signs in your yard – every one of you made a difference, and I’m forever grateful.

I also want to extend my deepest thanks to my wife Della, my family, and the volunteers that worked so hard to support me.

The ultimate mark of a strong democracy is to support the will of the voters, and while I was naturally working for a different result, I respect the people, the process and the system that’s vital to this country.

Thank you again for everything.

Joe Salerno

name: Jill Stein
subject: Another disastrous result from the two-party system
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jill Stein for President 2024
body:
Democrats have no one to blame but themselves for losing to Trump again.

| | | [ ](https://www.jillstein2024.com/?e=e813143e1dc0d3b0ffcb007ffe357333&utm_source=jillsteinforpresident&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=election_results_statement&n=1)
---
Peter, Once again the two-party system has delivered a disastrous result for the American people. Now we must continue the uprising for people-powered politics and demand the world we deserve - which will never be delivered by the twin parties of war and Wall Street. The Democrats have no one but themselves to blame for losing to Trump again, after their decades of failures and betrayals that paved the way for Trump’s rise in the first place. From their unconditional support for the endless war machine and genocide in Gaza, to their subservience to Wall Street, to their indifference to human rights, to their reckless acceleration of the fossil fuel pollution driving climate collapse, to their assault on democracy - on all these issues and many more the Democratic Party has betrayed the trust of the people. This election removes any remaining doubt that the Democratic Party has utterly failed the people it claims to represent, and that we need a genuine opposition party for people, planet, and peace.**** Over the last year, we have continued our long-term efforts to build the movement for peace, for a livable planet, and for all the countless millions of people in America and around the world who have been neglected and abused by the parties of war and Wall Street. The momentum we achieved despite the pervasive media blackout threatened the Democratic Party so much that they set up an unprecedented operation dedicated to the suppression of our grassroots movement. They harassed us with expensive lawsuits to kick us off the ballot, marginalized and smeared us in the media, posted jobs seeking people to sabotage our campaign, and even launched attack ads against us - something they’d never done before for any independent challenger.**** These unprecedented attacks against us by the corrupt political establishment backfired madly. These attacks inspired a whole new multiracial, multigenerational coalition of voters, organizers, and activists to join forces with the Green Party – and that coalition will continue to grow. Our campaign built relationships with powerhouse anti-genocide activists, especially in the Muslim and Arab American community; the Black community; the student movement; the reawakened antiwar movement; the radical labor movement; and many others.**** Year after year, millions of eligible voters have refused to vote for either establishment party, particularly young people, people of color, and people of lower income. In the 2020 presidential race, a full third of eligible voters declined to participate. These are exactly the people our agenda speaks to. The vast majority of voters are calling for a political alternative like the Green Party that serves the greater good instead of the warmongers and economic elites.**** The political establishment is quaking in their boots that the public is discovering us. They will be shocked to see how much our coalition grows as more voters discover they actually have a choice for the future they deserve.**** We have the solutions to end genocide and empire; to adopt a foreign policy based on international law, human rights and diplomacy; to tackle the climate emergency; to reverse wealth inequality and invest in human thriving; to dismantle systems of oppression and build a society rooted in justice, freedom, and equity.**** Our movement for people, planet, and peace is aligned with the values and priorities of the American people, and together we have the numbers we need to transform our future. We only have to get the word out to the millions of Americans who reject the failed status quo, and bring them into our movement.**** Everything we have achieved together is a victory for working people, for justice, for peace and a future where all of us can thrive.**** Thank you for your unwavering support and for joining in this fight to build an America and a world that works for all of us. We will only get stronger from here.**** The pressure will continue to build for the political transformation that the American people demand. The political elites will continue to tell you that resistance is futile. Ignore them. **This is our moment.****** Together, we are unstoppable.**** Jill
---
* * *
---

body:
President Trump has been through so much. He was put through sham trials, multiple attempts on his life, and relentless smears from the radical left and their allies in the media.
President Trump will MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN

|
---
| | | | [](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=cbb6e8472bbc4a4384f6d0507f88821c&_e=ujZPKIhl_5jXbxhTykpt6KOb-I8fkjUJnukWjFSff5cPqA4_djy-X6ZPdlWTDuNY8XVnbSXqfXDmLRSR8Uv8AdP8AicbzjZd2TzzIktR9tbyeWFfc2UNHyajfpDNl5U5R02PHv_YjLqfm1uH6FGyoP4cqEtt8IR_p6RKgcBYm1_P4CbgKYk--DajCpH8Jpe2I4WEh5yOtX7d3bU_Siz5MdAhBkg-XOfYfP8tqMGacsi9ptV0AP90Ruv-g1FFX9CwrC0tZS8031-5JaRbiW8a-94XSvjFFRKDH3lGGqeXbio6SqTLIkz0sxI2vl_3rBMa5d7JuNYeWHxbNQUxe--49PcISYhl_IPOZYW_7Zed24XlB1N_wOoNKSwGmgpj3gSAKP-PtV0uIhfSfiD2BL7xLwI3SneAX3AmUslD8gppmQc%3D)
---

The American people make it clear. It's time for Joe Biden and Kamala Harris to **_GET LOST!_**

**President Trump has been through so much. He was put through sham trials, multiple attempts on his life, and relentless smears from the radical left and their allies in the media.

But despite everything, with your help, he will return to the White House and Make America Great Again. **

**[That's why House Republicans are sending him a congratulations card, and we want this to be our biggest EVER. Will you be the first to add your name?](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=cbb6e8472bbc4a4384f6d0507f88821c&_e=ujZPKIhl_5jXbxhTykpt6KOb-I8fkjUJnukWjFSff5cPqA4_djy-X6ZPdlWTDuNY8XVnbSXqfXDmLRSR8Uv8AdP8AicbzjZd2TzzIktR9tbyeWFfc2UNHyajfpDNl5U5R02PHv_YjLqfm1uH6FGyoP4cqEtt8IR_p6RKgcBYm1_P4CbgKYk--DajCpH8Jpe2I4WEh5yOtX7d3bU_Siz5MdAhBkg-XOfYfP8tqMGacsi9ptV0AP90Ruv-g1FFX9CwrC0tZS8031-5JaRbiW8a-6MdRsXTUZqtTVajN8rZviFpi9ihfac54SMCO1X6g-PdKO_E0aEkaPDSHUvrCnTd607CGrmy212L30kN3avQXhIYMIGSeZ9-Od62Z01Ja3zLHtOX9v4gcQ1EtD2cKH10zqsrd51Ic3-3rumQ4YqxXzE%3D "That’s why House Republicans are sending him a congratulations card, and we want this to be our biggest EVER. Will you be the first to add your name?")**

clean_body: President Trump has been through so much. He was put through sham trials, multiple attempts on his life, and relentless smears from the radical left and their allies in the media. President Trump will MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN | | | | | The American people make it clear. It's time for Joe Biden and Kamala Harris to GET LOST! President Trump has been through so much. He was put through sham trials, multiple attempts on his life, and relentless smears from the radical left and their allies in the media. But despite everything, with your help, he will return to the White House and Make America Great Again. "That’s why House Republicans are sending him a congratulations card, and we want this to be our biggest EVER. Will you be the first to add your name?" SIGN HOUSE GOP 'S CARD TO PRESIDENT TRUMP | | | "Generic Button" President Trump and Republicans have a lot of work to do to get our country back on track, but we want to take a moment to congratulate President Trump on a hard-fought victory. "House Republicans are sending this card off today, so we need you to add your name ASAP." SIGN HOUSE GOP 'S CARD TO PRESIDENT TRUMP | | | "Generic Button" | | Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Team WFP
subject: Join us in community tomorrow night
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
| | | Peter, Donald Trump will be returning to the White House in 2025. That’s not a sentence any of us wanted to be reading this morning. It’s certainly not one we wanted to be writing. But it’s also not the end of the story. We’ll have more news to share with you soon about some inspiring WFP victories last night that point the way to the kind of country we want to build together. But right now — whether you are feeling anger or sadness, whether you’re ready to jump right back into the fight or need some time to process things — know that all of those feelings are valid. Take the time you need. Lean into the things and people that bring you joy and allow you to feel safe. **And, when you’re ready, the Working Families Party will be here as your political home as we move forward into 2025 and beyond.** That starts tomorrow, when WFP members will join in community with members of 200+ other organizations for a post-election call to help make sense of what happened, lay out concrete actions we can take this week, and start to chart a path forward together. [RSVP to join us for “Making Meaning of the Moment: Post-Election Mass Call” on Thursday, November 7 at 8:00 pm ET // 5:00 pm PT.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwneR9YUH85oX6XyKoEwATL_hbR3LKU0fNB3u-IwAuIzPNHzQiY0qkfZYRs2_9vcjg9p-rGlLnah40c4vTCmDw3dvwD5LjEoqGTo4Zt4kvDl99xbfYiYCEBbWYmSBQWwARCqnMXqTSGLbonTplR2BF8Jm5Yi7mKL-zPFfAECGGb368KJknT7gN0iuOWqX3nZAxzsB4Gs1ymQkSI2ncIvqSQszCRIR_3c61QxGWGpdAV0/4b8/p7ltMBHLRfOHU8q6JCxiGQ/h0/h001.vb6B7H44pBZSqjUKnZAdTHeJv1p8hY2Cxc14uz8GyjA) [](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwneR9YUH85oX6XyKoEwATL_hbR3LKU0fNB3u-IwAuIzPNHzQiY0qkfZYRs2_9vcj0JeSciCGZgEPwwwFdmLMVPAuZ8xkG6R4nMMHVvb0Js3CHDZ4wAWDPhkjYz0u2Ma47XDNjxJssfJnrjbL9zRBCm8S-xwl4bDr6Bu2Gkk9UZdDiw0spknqr6EPKPLNnk5eQ7Gje7LU172IOrNqbWT4owgSrDWYq4d4t5a0xdMYV0A/4b8/p7ltMBHLRfOHU8q6JCxiGQ/h1/h001.Dl2WtsaXAkYxWIom6-aH1MR3mzNK1rpXeLgwgaZ-lTI) **It may not feel like it right now, but today is the beginning of the end for the Trump era and the MAGA movement.** Donald Trump has no solutions to address the needs of working-class people in this country. He never has. And when he tries to implement his agenda of more tax cuts for billionaires, gutting health care, deporting millions, and supporting war crimes with public dollars, people will rise up. The solution to Trump’s politics of division is a politics of solidarity. We need to protect each other in the face of a right-wing authoritarian government. We need to join together to build the largest pro-democracy coalition in history and block Trump at every turn. **But it’s also clear from last night’s results that we’ll only get to victory by building a multiracial coalition of working-class people.** No one can know exactly what the future holds. But if we’re going to build a movement that will fight for each other over the next four years, we will all need to do our part. [Please join us for our post-election mass call on Thursday, November 7 at 8:00 pm ET // 5:00 pm PT.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMbGj-ca0TYSsYMZBvIweYdJxZAP5xLg1YZaKIinIFmIxwneR9YUH85oX6XyKoEwATL_hbR3LKU0fNB3u-IwAuIzPNHzQiY0qkfZYRs2_9vcj8KVgwOsMsAocw5aaDqyZCIBc0qAAvMGloZkBxiJXE-9t2BRFqqV30SjyJDN-QXcGyyClvzFOJy_imJLaA4QWjF1fr-vB7ramHwy-S0MX2mwYXVH57j_f9dsS6qpqTOsOYnZiqp5O3Ir_07l_Ahv8ko47rjdPjPUBRQvsBs6tX3c/4b8/p7ltMBHLRfOHU8q6JCxiGQ/h2/h001.U0y9b3C8nBV4LcHsDEFfxFv6_tFbVTmU5Bp___xQx2w) clean_body: | | | Peter, Donald Trump will be returning to the White House in 2025. That’s not a sentence any of us wanted to be reading this morning. It’s certainly not one we wanted to be writing. But it’s also not the end of the story. We’ll have more news to share with you soon about some inspiring WFP victories last night that point the way to the kind of country we want to build together. But right now — whether you are feeling anger or sadness, whether you’re ready to jump right back into the fight or need some time to process things — know that all of those feelings are valid. Take the time you need. Lean into the things and people that bring you joy and allow you to feel safe. And, when you’re ready, the Working Families Party will be here as your political home as we move forward into 2025 and beyond. That starts tomorrow, when WFP members will join in community with members of 200+ other organizations for a post-election call to help make sense of what happened, lay out concrete actions we can take this week, and start to chart a path forward together. It may not feel like it right now, but today is the beginning of the end for the Trump era and the MAGA movement. Donald Trump has no solutions to address the needs of working-class people in this country. He never has. And when he tries to implement his agenda of more tax cuts for billionaires, gutting health care, deporting millions, and supporting war crimes with public dollars, people will rise up. The solution to Trump’s politics of division is a politics of solidarity. We need to protect each other in the face of a right-wing authoritarian government. We need to join together to build the largest pro-democracy coalition in history and block Trump at every turn. But it’s also clear from last night’s results that we’ll only get to victory by building a multiracial coalition of working-class people. No one can know exactly what the future holds. But if we’re going to build a movement that will fight for each other over the next four years, we will all need to do our part. In solidarity, Team WFP Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. name: Leslie Mehta
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LESLIE MEHTA FOR CONGRESS • PO BOX 31404, HENRICO, VA 23294
body:
For believing in our cause, and supporting me every step of the way.

Leslie Mehta is a civil rights attorney, a mom, and a proud Democrat running to flip Virginia’s 1st Congressional District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/qilL9Kv-dUyGV2oDr4ABqg2?unsubscribedata=A%2BC9klXpgxoBz2Ktqt0gp8iu%2Bp5lNRh5tjrzgAmjkATdNovrauwud4ML5kLGUq88qyZDgSXJhr7iMyrz47EvnfiYF%2Fcw6V7%2B5Yqab6gRlP%2FKpRz6YiHIuyizREIDmtRD25pb%2FGhjSI6A%2BV%2BRZBhGFbxJ97RiwdONPrScRURr5wx855cjkMSoHtAcTBB%2B2EN7tMSfWdCD%2FmLZXZ7y1bjB0OUyJtjDMP3MtcYunOjFCVbHHdwFWRYHQsbiWSRwMvIiFCOHalR1g9PxtHK2LWXQ80vaAkqOQ6%2FqxVLmzVu%2B5yOCqJaNU7hE%2BuZBR725Y3CtJu%2BPSjh05ncnTDo%2BiFvGyZhSQCdeyV6brtV74sn0YzUFAF%2BdlVTgKT1OlvOSNANzbkd8wqyhVN2JRsXKpyHy8UjEhFW694bir1GvaGLZon9JswafNTJCYF8wuBoYY2Sj)
---
| |
---
Peter, When I launched my campaign for Congress in Virginia’s 1st earlier this year, I never expected to build a movement like the one we did. I never anticipated that so many folks from all walks of life would stand behind me in this fight for our district, our rights, and our future. Even though we didn’t get the results we were looking for last night, both in our race and in races up and down the ballot across the nation, I could not be prouder of the work that we did — together. That leaves me with one thing to say to you, Peter: **Thank you.** For believing in our cause, and supporting me every step of the way. What we did here is important work, and I couldn’t have done it without you. Before I ran for Congress, I defended your constitutional rights as Legal Director of the ACLU of Virginia, and then turned to healthcare advocacy to fight for my daughter Brooke. I raised awareness for rare diseases, advised Governor Northam AND Governor Youngkin on equity and medical issues and successfully petitioned Congress to set aside federal funding for Rett Syndrome and other rare diseases. **Though our campaign for Congress has ended, my advocacy work for all Virginians is far from over.** The road ahead may be a difficult one, but you can count on the fact that I’ll be fighting the good fight every step of the way. For my family, for yours, for Virginia, and for the values that we hold dear. There’s still so much left to do. Thank you, Peter. Together, Leslie Mehta

clean_body: For believing in our cause, and supporting me every step of the way. Leslie Mehta is a civil rights attorney, a mom, and a proud Democrat running to flip Virginia’s 1st Congressional District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Peter, When I launched my campaign for Congress in Virginia’s 1st earlier this year, I never expected to build a movement like the one we did. I never anticipated that so many folks from all walks of life would stand behind me in this fight for our district, our rights, and our future. Even though we didn’t get the results we were looking for last night, both in our race and in races up and down the ballot across the nation, I could not be prouder of the work that we did — together. That leaves me with one thing to say to you, Peter: Thank you. For believing in our cause, and supporting me every step of the way. What we did here is important work, and I couldn’t have done it without you. Before I ran for Congress, I defended your constitutional rights as Legal Director of the ACLU of Virginia, and then turned to healthcare advocacy to fight for my daughter Brooke. I raised awareness for rare diseases, advised Governor Northam AND Governor Youngkin on equity and medical issues and successfully petitioned Congress to set aside federal funding for Rett Syndrome and other rare diseases. Though our campaign for Congress has ended, my advocacy work for all Virginians is far from over. The road ahead may be a difficult one, but you can count on the fact that I’ll be fighting the good fight every step of the way. For my family, for yours, for Virginia, and for the values that we hold dear. There’s still so much left to do. Thank you, Peter. Together, Leslie Mehta | | | ---|---|--- Leslie Mehta is a civil rights attorney, a mom, and a proud Democrat running to flip Virginia’s 1st Congressional District. She believes in the Spider-Man definition of a law degree. With great power comes great responsibility. subject: Thank you, Peter

Peter,

**It’s the honor of my lifetime to continue serving our community in Washington.**

Thank you, Peter, for believing in me and standing with me every step of the way. _I know together we can continue this fight._

_Ritchie_
| | Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458
www.torres.nyc

Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.

# FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Peter,**I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns.

I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism.

**But there's also far more to what the Sun is building.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.

I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust.

The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored.

The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way.

**The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun.** A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.**My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly.**

I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths.

They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be. I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust. The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored. The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way. The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun. A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly. I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths. They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts. | | | | Please join me in the fight to restore America's journalism. This is one mission that our country desperately needs. | | | Larry Kudlow Columnist for The New York Sun | | | | | Larry Kudlow Columnist for The New York Sun | | |

This is not the result any of us wanted. I am sitting beside you all in anger, in frustration, and in grief.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/96698825/510884868/-93946384?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImY3N2Y4YjY1LTg3OWMtZWYxMS04OGNlLTAwMGQzYTk4ZmE2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=uo_KtiTNZ7j8fFtWJZmrZutlvUWqw3ayuQadBClxpxs=&emci=2509b501-799c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&emdi=f77f8b65-879c-ef11-88ce-000d3a98fa6b&ceid=920818)

| Peter,

This is not the result any of us wanted. I am sitting beside you all in anger, in frustration, and in grief.

I refuse, however, to just sit by and let Trump and his MAGA fanatics rule our country.

VP Harris and Governor Walz ran a historic campaign. Millions of Americans supported their message of hope – we thank them for inspiring us to envision a better America for everybody – however, this momentum does not go away. Emerge alums are still at the forefront and our state legislature win rate as of right now is 70%.

They have inspired so many to pick up the torch and continue their work. We have seen this before, especially at Emerge after 2016. Emerge will continue to work, recruiting and training the next generation of Emerge candidates and providing them with the tools to run and win.

_**If you are fueled by this to run for office and are considering taking the next step, Emerge is here for you.**_

name: Quentin Wathum-Ocama, Young Democrats of America via yda.org
subject: Statement on the Results of the 2024 Presidential Election
body:
| | | | | | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsGRA_J6jtCqYTGf9TBARmggSPVbT9acGv-q3fuGkH5fpWKqqPlmD81G2Ja6cSXR_GQGr7irGGwY2TUsaA4tbOwnS_oVl-SCOjaRBBb7gBAOG9Z_D2-VrUhbjSbtGz2uvmTKXFvDLwhRYcRs4h08kxX_gM9564vln777wJuqfZ_6E-5MsnPUmNPKv39JQZ3X-UuxWRtbeL9ndwLP_bz_bwfx_rsUAim1TyHS8mzbcibuhjVScqwdGSlHrENcb_vTCRha0w25aygXsWDxOjXqpIKi3UPj917p7VhXoVSwUpPCzjePBzewaSM3MsiGSZmmtg7ATUehssibna_XkV1UzEMJtPzvu4rDzb-t16vXvyjV69IZSE5X20tRh7IT_il4MM/4b8/VqPb7LVZRX2rgWgTJ0D60A/h0/h001.QtWdUj2o2IVCT93w4vx3Dl0arUq-0zKIlA0tvBF1SvQ)
---
---
---
While this is not the outcome we had hoped for, we will not give up. For the sake of women, immigrants, the LGBTQ+ community, religious minorities, and working families in the United States and around the world, we will continue to fight back against oppression, bigotry, and hate.

I commend Vice President Harris for running a campaign unlike anything we’ve seen before. She is the first Black woman and Asian American to lead a major party ticket, raising over $1 billion since taking over in July and fighting like hell for what she stands for. We also thank President Biden and Vice President Harris for their commitment to a peaceful transfer of power.
**This is not the end.** Make no mistake: the next four years will be exhausting, depressing, and critical to the survival of our democracy. **Young Democrats across the nation will continue organizing from the ground level and working towards a better future.**
I encourage every young person upset with these results to use those feelings to help drive change. Every young person who hasn’t already should go to _[yda.org](www.yda.org?link_id=1&can_id=12471c8e444f80358077c9724aea85a0&source=email-my-election-day-message-2&email_referrer=email_2521970&email_subject=statement-on-the-results-of-the-2024-presidential-election)_ today to find out how to connect with their local Young Democrats chapter and help organize much-needed resistance against the incoming Trump Administration’s anti-American agenda.
**Together, we will build a better, stronger, and more resilient Democratic Party—one prepared to meet the challenges ahead and to stand firm in defense of our shared values.**
**We are all in this together.**

Quentin Wathum-Ocama
President, Young Democrats of America
| **_**The Young Democrats of America (YDA) is the official youth wing of the Democratic Party of the United States and the oldest partisan youth organization in the country. To help us mobilize the next generation of Democrats,**[donate anything you can afford right now to YDA.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvpvx43AV9gSahrX7Vza7T_RPaLyq8Fmvx7JUU3Z5JlokkqYQWbGMUEfDi-tfZKqnglUT6ndyDTOLcaJVR6mPVqY60JPv7nM-iARL6BRf1K3yA6XjjqU7Kfq76vC47j9KPCWQhF9eYc_QdaO8rzIuFgkTUWhrn_Oxxmoo6FVTpgSJY3MSCo6SAkkCKA6rpBmt35pd6agwYVslxbU_nkoFeMD0nQk2hZI7n4Vtdv8ggV-SQ6RgBBwHRMs0OCjBefLllrOzit4UZ6CkxEEOEVo8xSWT16n6_dwxpm46dbfMxoXYc4O-K_w4IxsKrNjs33lssW4jx1ORCN-WXxMF4VC-vITAyhdiG1m6Fcv55slpzA7A/4b8/VqPb7LVZRX2rgWgTJ0D60A/h1/h001.ilg8HHqqnAFM8lAbkhxMxru2MmswPazHo69jy728Huo)_**

clean_body: | | | | | | While this is not the outcome we had hoped for, we will not give up. For the sake of women, immigrants, the LGBTQ+ community, religious minorities, and working families in the United States and around the world, we will continue to fight back against oppression, bigotry, and hate. I commend Vice President Harris for running a campaign unlike anything we’ve seen before. She is the first Black woman and Asian American to lead a major party ticket, raising over $1 billion since taking over in July and fighting like hell for what she stands for. We also thank President Biden and Vice President Harris for their commitment to a peaceful transfer of power. This is not the end. Make no mistake: the next four years will be exhausting, depressing, and critical to the survival of our democracy. Young Democrats across the nation will continue organizing from the ground level and working towards a better future. I encourage every young person upset with these results to use those feelings to help drive change. Every young person who hasn’t already should go to www.yda.org?linkid=1&canid=12471c8e444f80358077c9724aea85a0&source=email-my-election-day-message-2&emailreferrer=email2521970&emailsubject=statement-on-the-results-of-the-2024-presidential-election today to find out how to connect with their local Young Democrats chapter and help organize much-needed resistance against the incoming Trump Administration’s anti-American agenda. Together, we will build a better, stronger, and more resilient Democratic Party—one prepared to meet the challenges ahead and to stand firm in defense of our shared values. We are all in this together. Quentin Wathum-Ocama President, Young Democrats of America | The Young Democrats of America (YDA) is the official youth wing of the Democratic Party of the United States and the oldest partisan youth organization in the country. Peter, the ballots have been cast & the votes have been tallied - I won re-election to the TN State Legislature!
Whether you spread the word to friends & family, volunteered, donated, or supported our race in any way - thank you. You have made the difference in this race , and it means the world to have your support.
Now the work begins to ensure meaningful action is taken in the 2025 legislative session. Thanks to our incredible supporters, I can continue advocating for the best interests of our district, city, and state in the TN State Senate.
Our fight continues, but today we celebrate this win and remind ourselves that when we stand together, progress is possible in Tennessee.
Heidi Campbell
Senator Heidi Campbell is running for re-election in TN Senate District 20. She is championing change for Tennessee and won't rest until we see gun safety reform, corporate accountability, and a safe and prosperous community where everyone can thrive.

name: Gabby Giffords
subject: The news we woke up to today
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
I’m disappointed by the result of the election, but like Vice President Harris, I accept it.
#

Dear friends,
I’m disappointed by the result of the election, but like Vice President Harris, I accept it. Here in America, the rule of law and will of the voters must come before preference for any candidate or political party. No one—including the president of the United States—is above the law. Not now, not ever.
To those anxious about what the future holds, I understand. After being shot in the head and nearly killed, it would have been easy to lose hope—but I never did. Despite all the pain, I held onto my hopes for this country every bit as tightly as I did the hope that I would one day take a walk or talk with my family. I learned that trying times bring out the power of the human spirit.
I also saw how the support of others—family and strangers alike—can be transformative. So, now is the moment for us to stand together. Link arms with your friends and neighbors who are immigrants, LGBTQ+, survivors of gun violence, Black, Brown, Muslim or Jewish. As my friend and personal hero Congressman John Lewis used to say, we ‘must be bold, brave, and courageous and find a way to get in the way.’ Now is the time for taking a stand—together—against division, against hatred, and against the erosion of truth. And to women everywhere: I won’t rest until every one of our rights and freedoms has been restored.
My friend Kamala Harris ran a great campaign on a short timeline, overcoming challenges that no other candidate in recent memory had encountered. It was inspiring to see people of all ages excited by a candidate running on kindness toward fellow Americans and optimism for the future. While this isn’t the result we wanted, I am proud of the hard work so many people put into this campaign.
All of us must work just as hard these next four years.
[**And so my dear friends, if you’re ready to stand with me, and to fuel the work to come for the next four years and beyond in our movement for gun safety, I’m asking you to make a donation to GIFFORDS PAC today.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/14457?c0=giffords-em-nov24.MBs8nS&refcode=em241106-1-30&t=1&akid=9387%2E3935704%2EpnX-4_)

We’re going to attempt to FLIP HUNDREDS of school board seats from LIBERAL to CONSERVATIVE in 2025, and we desperately need proud patriots like you to join our team to secure America’s future! name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: Onward
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jocelyn Benson for Secretary of State
body:
Friend,
As we reflect on the events of the last 24 hours, I want to remind you all that democracy has never risen or fallen based on the actions of any one leader. It’s always thrived only when the American people demand that it does.
So our charge, for all of us in the years ahead, will be to continue to stand firm and speak out against anyone, no matter how powerful, who tries to take away our voices, our votes, our freedom, and our democracy. I’ll continue to proudly wave that flag and march into battle with that as our charge.
I hope we also all realize and are grateful for the tireless work of election officials over the course of this year and these last several weeks. These professionals — be they Democratic, Republican or Independent — are the best of us. May we all continue to stand with them and ensure we remain in a country that lives up to that one person, one vote promise embedded in our Constitution. May none of us give up on that ideal. It is who we are as a nation, and as a republic.
Onward.
Jocelyn Benson
Secretary of State

* * *

Before you go... we need your help. Since her 2018 election, Secretary Benson has fought for free and fair elections. She’s on the front lines of defending and strengthening our democracy. clean_body: Friend, As we reflect on the events of the last 24 hours, I want to remind you all that democracy has never risen or fallen based on the actions of any one leader. It’s always thrived only when the American people demand that it does. So our charge, for all of us in the years ahead, will be to continue to stand firm and speak out against anyone, no matter how powerful, who tries to take away our voices, our votes, our freedom, and our democracy. I’ll continue to proudly wave that flag and march into battle with that as our charge. I hope we also all realize and are grateful for the tireless work of election officials over the course of this year and these last several weeks. These professionals — be they Democratic, Republican or Independent — are the best of us. May we all continue to stand with them and ensure we remain in a country that lives up to that one person, one vote promise embedded in our Constitution. May none of us give up on that ideal. It is who we are as a nation, and as a republic. Onward. Jocelyn Benson Secretary of State Before you go... we need your help. Since her 2018 election, Secretary Benson has fought for free and fair elections. She’s on the front lines of defending and strengthening our democracy. = = Paid for by Jocelyn Benson for Secretary of State Mailing Address: PO BOX 21368, Detroit, MI 48221. name: Elissa Slotkin
subject: We won
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
body:
# [](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/188?t=1&akid=2194%2E135344%2Ef_3UUH)

Hi friend,
Today has been a day of powerful and mixed emotions for me -- and I would imagine, for many of you -- as we process last night’s results. But I wanted to share a moment of brightness and gratitude with you: We won.
My race has officially been called, and I am so honored to be the next Senator from the great state of Michigan. Here’s a link to the speech I gave just moments before the results were called: **[https://www.youtube.com/live/pnZDasKPFps?t=604s](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3219?t=2&akid=2194%2E135344%2Ef_3UUH)**
[](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3219?t=3&akid=2194%2E135344%2Ef_3UUH)
**[WATCH HERE](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/3219?t=4&akid=2194%2E135344%2Ef_3UUH)**
We’ll have a more detailed analysis of our race to share this week, but for now I just want to start by saying thank you. This would not have happened without the hard work and support of so many: my family, my campaign manager Matt Hennessey and the team he assembled, the great Senator Debbie Stabenow, and most of all, the voters of Michigan. Not only those who voted for me, but also all of the voters across the state who took the time to make their voice heard.
It has been a tremendous honor to appear on your ballot for U.S. Senator and a privilege to travel across the state and meet so many of you.
While the overall results last night were far from everything we had hoped for, I urge you now more than ever to stay engaged. Take time, grieve, and feel your feelings, but please do not give in to despair.
We will navigate this moment together, like the generations before us who fought for our rights and our freedoms, faced setbacks, but ultimately made life better for us.
You have heard me talk about engaged citizens and principled leaders — we need both now more than ever, and I will do everything in my power to be the principled leader you deserve.
Thank you for all that you have done for me and our state,
Elissa
**[DONATE](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/go/9?c0=ebs-em-nov24.k4yPYt&refcode=em241106-1-900&t=5&akid=2194%2E135344%2Ef_3UUH)**

PAID FOR BY ELISSA SLOTKIN FOR MICHIGAN
P.O. Box 4145
East Lansing, MI 48826
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=6&akid=2194%2E135344%2Ef_3UUH).

[ Sign up here to receive fewer emails.](https://act.elissaslotkin.org/signup/users-fewer-emails/?t=7&akid=2194%2E135344%2Ef_3UUH)

Elissa Slotkin served in the Central Intelligence Agency and the Department of Defense. Use of her job titles and photographs during service do not imply endorsement by the Central Intelligence Agency OR the Department of Defense.
